name: Holden Center for Leadership & Community Engagement
description: Take a moment to stop and ask yourself some of life's harder questions. Who am I? What am I doing here? Where am I going? Not easy questions to contemplate. We believe that the journey to answering these questions is just as important as finding the answers. In order to find them, you must engage with the life and people around you. You must identify your strengths, connect with your passions, and envision the world that you want to live in. In short, we need people who are leaders. We need you. Don't believe us? Challenge accepted. Join us and we'll show you how to be the leader that you never dreamed you could be. We're located on the ground floor of the Erb Memorial Union, office space 047. You can access our center by the amphitheater entrance.
summary: We need people who are leaders. We need you. Don't believe us? Challenge accepted. Join us and we'll show you how to be the leader that you never dreamed you could be. We're located on the ground floor of the Erb Memorial Union, office space 047.

name: Align Magazine
description: Align is an application-based magazine and club that gives UO students with a love of art, culture and fashion a chance to be published. We pride ourselves in featuring work that emboldens creativity and showcases artists within our community. We open applications at the beginning of every term, all are welcome to apply! Follow @align_mag on Instagram to see application updates.
summary: We are an arts and culture magazine with an emphasis on fashion and lifestyle. We exist to form and foster creative community on campus, build up students' creative portfolios and publish great work!

name: Associated Graduate Students in Geography
description:
The Associated Graduate Students in Geography (AGSG) at the University of Oregon strives to maintain a productive environment for graduate students in the Department of Geography, and to enhance the Department. By helping strengthen the Geography department’s graduate program and supporting the progression of current students, Associated Graduate Students in Geography hopes to contribute in positive ways to the university’s growing success and recognition as a leading research institution.

 

The goal of the Associated Graduate Students in Geography is to provide leadership, organization, and represent the interest of the geography graduate student community. Every first Monday of the month (during or after 608), AGSG will meet to discuss present and long-term goals and needs of the graduate students in the department and plan events that contribute to the knowledge, experience, and team building among graduate students. The discussions in the monthly AGSG meetings will be communicated to the faculty, as appropriate, by the Graduate Representatives serving as the Faculty Chairs. Taking measures to support graduate work, discussing opportunities, and providing a space for effective professional development are the Association’s primary endeavors.
summary: The goal of the Associated Graduate Students in Geography is to provide leadership, organization, and represent the interest of the geography graduate student community.

name: University Theatre
description:
The mission of the University Theatre is two-fold. One is to provide an opportunity for University of Oregon students to participate in dramatic arts productions. These opportunities serve as a laboratory for Theatre Arts majors to apply what they learn in classes, but participation is not limited to majors only. All University of Oregon students are welcome to take part and may serve a production as actors, directors, designers, choreographers, playwrights, composers, musicians, technicians, dramaturgs, publicists, and arts managers.

The second mission of the University Theatre is to provide an opportunity for University of Oregon students and residents of the community and surrounding area to see live theatrical performances of high caliber at affordable prices. The availability of cultural events such as University Theatre productions is an important resource for the students attending the University.

Everything you wanted to know about Theatre Arts: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1SDbd0_DlY6tZql5Oicn3i0VgJqKDZA5j/view?usp=sharing
summary: University Theatre is here to provide an opportunity for UO students to participate in dramatic arts productions as well as for UO students and community members to see live theatrical performances of high caliber at affordable prices.

name: Voices of Change for Child Safety
description:
Before requesting membership on Engage, please fill out our interest form: 

https://oregon.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_d6m9miJa2yr2HFc

 

Voices of Change for Childhood Safety (VOICES) is a University of Oregon student group that promotes primary child abuse prevention through education and engagement with our campus community. Our efforts are guided by the belief that child maltreatment is preventable. We advance our mission through the promotion of advocacy and education among students. 

VOICES is currently the only UO student organization solely dedicated to peer-led child abuse prevention education and advocacy. As a bridge between students and the Center for Childhood Safety and Wellbeing, the group fills a vital gap in on-campus efforts for child safety and wellness. We recognize that the subject matter we tackle may be difficult and, as such, members are expected to invite respectful and supportive dialogue in our spaces.
summary: Voices of Change for Childhood Safety partners with the College of Education's Center for Childhood Safety and Wellbeing to promote primary prevention efforts among the student body and advance child wellbeing.

name: Coalition for Indigenous Rights and Environmental Justice
description: CIREJ is a collection of UO students and faculty working in collaboration with Bolivian Indigenous communities, both in person and remotely, to support native rights and environmental justice. The organization is connected to the GEO "Indigenous Rights and Environmental Justice in Bolivia" study-abroad program; CIREJ's founding members were participants of the 2019 trip. However, the group is open to any who wish to participate and not solely study-abroad program participants. Together with indigenous community members and professors in Bolivia, CIREJ develops projects such as website creation, marketing assistance, and publicity creation which benefit the communities. Additionally, CIREJ is working with GEO to offer additional support to students preparing to participate in the "Indigenous Rights and Environmental Justice in Bolivia" study-abroad program, such as team-building exercises and camera-use training.
summary: University of Oregon community members working collaboratively with Indigenous communities in Bolivia to support indigenous rights and environmental justice.

name: Women in STEM
description: Women in STEM (WIS) empowers and assists undergraduate female-identifying and nonbinary students in STEM majors/minors or with interest in STEM. We encompass all STEM interests, from biology and chemistry to math and physics. We also recognize the importance of science education and communication to research and for the development of the next generation of scientists. Our club’s goals are to guide students to succeed in their classes, help students apply for and receive scholarships and internships, and build a sense of community amongst UO women in STEM. We host weekly meetings throughout the academic year, with past events including presentations from professors, grad student panels, lab tours, mental health nights, and workshops about how to get involved in research.
summary: We are a club of students interested in STEM fields that creates opportunities for female-identifying people to access STEM resources and encourage a love of STEM.

name: Student Alumni Leadership
summary: Student Alumni Leadership helps lead the strategic direction of the Student Alumni Association, an organization of students led by students that maintains University of Oregon traditions, inspires commitment in students, and serves the greater community.

name: American English Institute
description: Since 1978, the American English Institute (AEI) has been a leader in English language teaching, research, and service on the University of Oregon (UO) campus and around the world. The American English Institute, as a unit a of the College of Arts and Sciences, serves the educational mission of the University of Oregon through scholarship, English language instruction, and English language professional training. The AEI champions the University’s mission of internationalization and is a leader in helping achieve the University’s goals for diversity and student success. The AEI supports the University in the integration of international students into all aspects of campus life, and collaborates with faculty across campus toward creating a successful academic experience for all international students.
summary: University of Oregon's English as a Second Language School catered for international students looking to strengthen their English and develop the skills needed to be a successful student here at U of O.

name: Jewish Law Student Association
description: The Jewish Law Student Association (JLSA) at the University of Oregon School of Law seeks to provide a relaxed and welcoming community for Jewish law students, and any student who wishes to learn more about and participate in different facets of Jewish culture. Membership is casual, and the group is open to University of Oregon students in the law, LLM, and CRES programs. We plan to invite students to join us for different events like lunchtime meetings, Shabbat dinners, holiday celebrations and educational programs. Through these events, we aim to foster an atmosphere of learning, promote inclusion, and engage in meaningful dialogue about Judaism and Jewish culture.
summary: The Jewish Law Student Association (JLSA) at the University of Oregon School of Law provides a community for Jewish law students, and all students who wish to learn more about Jewish culture.

name: Board And Card Gamers' Association for Maximum Nerdery
description:

summary: The university is committed to ensuring the health and safety of all our students. As such, we seek to prevent the abuse of alcohol and illegal drugs.

name: Graduate Women in Business
description: Graduate Women in Business was founded as a place for female, femme and non-binary students to network, socialize and learn in a safe space. Graduate Women in Business hosts speakers and trainings to support our future careers as well as informal social gatherings to build our community.
summary: Association of women graduate students studying business.

name: Moot Court
description:
The Moot Court Board (MCB) at the University of Oregon School of Law is dedicated to providing students with opportunities to develop and refine essential legal skills. Our goal is to enhance students’ critical thinking, research, and persuasive writing skills, as well as to encourage teamwork, leadership, and professional development. With the assistance and mentorship of practicing attorneys, we strive to equip our members with the knowledge, skills, and confidence necessary to succeed in the legal profession.

 

View our promotional video for more information!
summary: The Moot Court Board is a law student organization that provides students with opportunities to practice their written and oral advocacy, connect with and learn from the practicing bar, and develop practical legal skills.

name: Oregon Student Association
summary: The Oregon Student Association (OSA) is a statewide, student-led advocacy and organizing nonprofit. OSA was established in 1975 to represent, serve, and protect the collective interests of students in post-secondary education in Oregon.

name: Affiliated Students for Undergraduate Research and Engagement
description:
The Affiliated Students for Undergraduate Research and Engagement (ASURE) is a student group that was founded to help connect students with research happening at the University of Oregon.

ASURE aims to be the voice of the student body interested in participating in research. With the mission of being the one-stop-shop for peers on campus to be able to find advice, resources, and opportunities ASURE has grown to provide workshops, classroom visits, and advice to students interested in getting involved in research and faculty interested in working with undergraduates.
summary: The Affiliated Students for Undergraduate Research and Engagement (ASURE)  is a student group that was founded to help connect students with research happening at the University of Oregon.

name: Out in STEM
description:
oSTEM @ UO is a chapter of the national oSTEM society dedicated to educating and fostering leadership for LGBTQQIA communities in the STEM fields.

 

As an organization dedicated to community, it serves as a touchstone and reminds us of our common goals: to educate, empower, and engage a diverse community, to identify, address, and advocate for the needs of LGBTQIA+ students in the STEM fields, and fulfill these needs through mentorship connections, networking opportunities, strategic collaborations, and professional development at The University of Oregon and in local communities.
summary: Out in Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (oSTEM) at the University of Oregon is a safe and supportive environment for LGBTQQIA+ students and allies to network, collaborate, and participate in professional development opportunities.

name: Law & Entrepreneurship Student Association
description:
LESA provides a forum for discussion of business and entrepreneurship while supporting students as they pursue a career in business law. 

 

https://www.instagram.com/uolesa/
summary: The Law & Entrepreneurship Student Association (LESA) is the primary business law organization at the University of Oregon School of Law. We are devoted to connecting UO students with the business law community while welcoming those of all demographics.

name: Global Affairs Society FKA United Nations Association
description:
In an incredibly changing world, The Global Affairs Society values local and global engagement, promoting rich discussion, and advocacy for change surrounding international policy, including that of the UN. We believe in the interconnectedness of local and global communities and aim to invest in the strength of youth engagement and activism as a means of developing that relationship. 

Everyone is welcome!

------------

Winter Term Meeting Time: Wednesday 6-7pm

Week 2: Geopolitics of the Arctic

Week 4: Nuclear Energy

Week 6: Venezuela/US Relations

Week 8: Guest Speaker

------------

Our club chooses to engage through student led discussion of global issues, UN policy, social justice, and career development. We host rich discussions and invite guest speakers to engage in international topics as well as provide social networking opportunities!

------------

Check out our Instagram page for the most current information!

Email gas.uoregon@gmail.com or visit @gas.uoregon on Instagram to request to be placed on our mailing list or if looking for more information!
summary: GAS is a student organization to discuss global issues, UN policy, social justice, and career development. We host rich discussions and invite guest speakers to engage in international issues and provide social networking opportunities!

name: Album Listening Club at UO
description: A club for lovers of all things music! We hold weekly occurring events in McKenzie 240A at 6 pm on Wednesdays. We discuss a weekly assigned album/genre and share our thoughts on what we liked, disliked, and what music we've been listening to lately. We also hold events throughout the year like listening parties, physical media swaps, and more. The purpose of the Album Listening Club at the University of Oregon is to create a safe and friendly space for students to meet other music fans, develop friendships, and engage in conversations. ALC at UO is a place to share your love and admiration for all things music, whether it's music production, songwriting, niche subgenres, or anything in between!
summary: A club for lovers of all things music! Every week, we meet up to discuss a weekly assigned album/genre and share our thoughts on what we've liked, disliked, and what music we've been listening to lately.

name: Oregon Blockchain Group
description: Oregon Blockchain Group's mission is to connect students with industry experts and professionals in blockchain. We provide comprehensive education on blockchain technology, covering technical and non-technical aspects and showcasing its real-world applications. Our members gain valuable experience through networking trips, conferences, socials, and support for student-led projects and research. Through our node operations, consulting for real companies, and venture capital activities, we empower students to engage deeply with the blockchain industry and build meaningful, hands-on skills.
summary: Oregon Blockchain aims to educate students on blockchain and help students network both on and off campus. We will provide resources and opportunities for students to learn from faculty, other students, experts, and industry professionals.

name: Bioethics Society
description:
The Bioethics Society is for students interested in the ethical and humanities aspects of science, technology, and medicine. With continuous advances in science and medicine, our world faces pressing ethical questions that determine the future of these fields. This club strives to provoke conversation among these topics through conversations, learning, guest speakers, projects, and more. Students interested in medicine, global health, research, technology, or many other fields will find this club a great place to look into an alternative perspective in these fields. Keywords [ethics, medicine, health, STEM, science, biology, technology].

https://linktr.ee/uobioethicssociety
summary: Bioethics Society is for students interested in the impact of science on humanity and our environment. We strive to provoke conversation and learning about the ethical issues of the life sciences through discussions, guest speakers, and volunteering!

name: Student Advocacy Program
description:
The ASUO Student Advocacy Program is a service funded by the ASUO Student Government and operated by Student Government Engagement and Success.

We offer help, support, and referrals to any student or student group involved in a dispute with the University.

  • We serve students accused of behavioral issues or violations the Student Code of Conduct.
  • We work with students on petitions so they may effectively advocate on behalf of their disputes related to housing, financial aid, and accessibility needs.
  • We guide students through informal & formal resolutions for grievances with students, faculty, and University policy.

We closely partner with the Office of Investigations and Civil Rights Compliance, the Office of Student Conduct and Community Standards, Office of Financial Aid and Scholarships, and other administrators.
summary: The ASUO Student Advocacy Program is a service funded by the ASUO Student Government and operated by Student Government Engagement and Success.

name: Star Wars Club at UO
description:
Welcome to Star Wars Club at the University of Oregon!

Our mission is to foster a positive environment for all types of Star Wars fans, no matter the content you prefer or your race, gender, or sexuality. We want to talk and share our love of the franchise with YOU!

We have weekly meetings on Thursdays for an hour in the evening. Pay attention to the Discord and the Instagram page for updates, as the location and exact times can change each term. We also have the occasional special event coming up, so stay tuned for those as well!

Meetings include game nights, discussions, general hang-outs and plenty of other activities for you to get to know the Star Wars community at UO better. We’d love for you to join us!

May The Force Be With You!
summary: Come to weekly meetings at Star Wars club if you love to talk about all things happening in a galaxy far, far away! We have discussions, games, debates, and celebrations! All grades and knowledge levels welcome!

name: Duck Samba
description:
Duck Samba is a club centered around the appreciation, study, and performance of the samba traditions of Brazil. We currently play Rio Style samba-enredo, and some Bahian samba-reggae. No prior samba or musical experience is necessary. All are welcome! We are always looking to expand our repertoire and reach. We have played for UO women's soccer and UO women's basketball. We also played at the Prefontaine Classic. Hoping to get more involved in the future!

Below is a link to some of our current repertoire. 

https://youtu.be/kNH7bBtBGGI

name: Women in PreHealth Science
description: Welcome to the Women in Pre-Health Sciences Club at the University of Oregon! Our group of women works alongside one another to achieve our goals in the pre-health fields, and prepare for post-grad. We lovingly welcome all marginalized gender/sex identities and allies. We have a place for all people who are interested the pre-health field especially people looking to go to medical school, PA school, nursing school, veterinarian school, dental school, optometry school, and all other graduate programs for nutritionists, occupational therapists, and athletic trainers. We hope to see new and familiar faces this winter term!
summary: Women in Pre-Health Sciences' club gives women a place to grow together and support one another while providing a chain of professional connections, study sessions, and other opportunities to ease the transition from College into Health Fields.

name: Student Parent Child Subsidy
description:
Update February 2026:

The 2025-26 Childcare Subsidy Program is currently FULL. We encourage prospective participants to apply to be placed on out waitlist. If additional funding becomes available, students on the waitlist will be admitted in the order their applications were received. Students who will be enrolled between Summer 2026 and Spring 2027 are welcome to apply for the 2026-27 program year; the application will open in July 2026.

For assistance identifying other childcare financial resources for which you may be eligible, visit our website or reach out to us at subsidy@uoregon.edu or 541-346-0632.

 

Do not request to become a "member" of this Engage group since membership in Engage is not part of our application, claim, or communication processes. See the instructions below for how to apply for this subsidy.

 

2025-26 Application

The 2025-26 Childcare Subsidy program year runs from July 2025 through June 2026. The application is open as of 8AM on Tuesday, July 8th.

Students who participated in the 2024-25 Childcare Subsidy program, please do not attempt to submit claims for July expenses using the 2024-25 claims form. A new, 2025-26 claims form will be provided to you when you are accepted into the 2025-26 subsidy program.

This program will not reach capacity during summer 2025. Additionally, the income qualification requirement is being removed starting with the 2025-26 program year. If you are registered for any number of credits in the current term or the subsequent term, your application will be approved. Once approved, you will submit claims for reimbursement. You will be eligible for reimbursement of expenses incurred during any academic quarter in which you were assessed an Incidental Fee (regardless of the number of credits you took).

 

About

The UO Childcare Subsidy helps student parents achieve their educational objectives by covering some of the costs associated with the care of their child or children. It reimburses a portion of child care expenses up to a maximum based on each child's age. 

 

How to Apply

1. If you're not already signed in with your DuckID, look for the "Sign In" button in the top right corner of this page

2. Sign in using your DuckID

3. Scroll down to the Forms section below

4. Choose the form "Child Care Subsidy Application Form YY-YY [start here]"

 

Eligibility  

If you are registered for any number of credits in the current term or the subsequent term, your application will be approved. Once approved, you will submit claims for reimbursement. You will be eligible for reimbursement of expenses incurred during any academic quarter in which you were assessed an Incidental Fee (regardless of the number of credits you took). The program does not require that students be U.S. citizens or permanent residents; it is also available to international students. See the Program Guidelines for full eligibility criteria. 

 

Program Guidelines 

Please review the Childcare Subsidy Program Guidelines before applying to participate.

 

Other Resources  

To learn about other basic needs resources on your UO campus and in the communities of Eugene/Springfield and Portland, visit the Basic Needs Program website.
summary: Childcare Subsidy Program

name: JStreetU
description: J Street U is the student organizing arm of J Street, the political home for pro-democracy, pro-peace Americans dedicated to ensuring a lasting peace in Israel and Palestine. We are a nationwide movement of campus chapters advocating and educating on colleges and universities, in our communities and on Capitol Hill for vigorous and sustained American leadership in facilitating a negotiated, two-state resolution to the Israeli-Palestinian conflict. We are deeply committed to our pro-peace, pro-democracy, anti-war, and pro-humanity ideals while establishing political, civil, and human rights for all Palestinians and Israelis.
summary: J Street U Is a student run organization focused on education and advocacy of the Israel-Palestine Conflict, with a commitment to seeking a two-state solution in the region and ensuring a lasting peace.

name: Magic the Gathering Club
description:
Welcome to Magic: the Gathering club! We are a group of people looking to play/talk/trade anything that has to do with the popular trading card game Magic! Feel free to email us with any questions or concerns you may have and if you're interested in joining, please join our Discord. All skill levels from beginner to competitive players are welcome! Left your collection at home or just wanting to learn the game? Don't worry! Through regular events like karaoke, trivia, and bingo nights, our club has become the talk of the town and a fixture of the UOregon campus. You can find us on Instagram at @oregontssociety where we post regular updates about our events each term.

name: Keyboard DucKeys fka MTNA Collegiate chapter at the University of Oregon
description: Keyboard DucKeys is a piano performance and pedagogy-focused collegiate chapter co-sponsored by the Music Teachers National Association. Also known as ‘MTNA Ducks,’ this group is dedicated to connecting music students in the UO community to teachers in the Oregon area, and nationwide. summary: Keyboard DucKeys is a piano performance and pedagogy-focused collegiate chapter co-sponsored by the Music Teachers National Association. This group is dedicated to connecting music students in the UO community to teachers in the Oregon area.

name: UO Chapter of the Hispanic Public Relations Association
description:
Welcome to The UO Chapter of the Hispanic Public Relations Student Association (Unidos). We exist to provide a space for the LatinX community who share an interest in strategic communication to come together, network and develop professional communication skills. You only need to have an interest in communication to join.

 

The Hispanic Public Relations Association nationally recognizes Unidos as the 2nd student chapter in the U.S. We take pride in the name Unidos as this club is a place where every SOJC major is welcomed. These industries are merging more every day, and it's our job to provide a space where we can practice working together to become better future PR, advertising and journalism professionals. 

 

Contact us via DM on Instagram @unidosatuo, sending an email to our president at agarci21@uoregon.edu or contacting our club advisor and director of the PR department, Dean Mundy @dmundy@uoregon.edu.
summary: The UO Chapter of the Hispanic Public Relations Student Association (Unidos) exists as a space for the LatinX community to share an interest in strategic communication to come together, network and develop professional communication skills

name: Criminal Defense Association
description: The CDA is the student chapter of the Oregon Criminal Defense Lawyer's Association at UO Law! Join us to learn about all things criminal law, network with your peers and lawyers, and participate in fun events. summary: The CDA is the student chapter of the Oregon Criminal Defense Lawyer's Association at UO Law! Join us to learn about all things criminal law, network with your peers and lawyers, and participate in fun events.

name: Science of the Good Life - Positive Psychology Club
description:
The Positive Psychology Club aims to cultivate a loving and supportive community that inspires well-being through learning and practicing science-based tools and techniques. Some of our past meetings include the Psychology of Music, Friendship, and Gratitude. We have done events such as Sound Bath Meditations, Warm Tea and Walk to Washburn Park, Jamba Juice & Journaling, and Yoga. Other meetings we invite in guest speakers who share about their expertise such as EFT Tapping, Q&A with Psychologist, and EMDR. 

Check out our website to learn more about past and upcoming meetings, our amazing leaders, and positive psychology topics we cover.

Website: www.uopositivepsych.org

Follow our Instagram or join our Teams chat to get information and reminders about upcoming meetings!

Positive Psych Club Instagram

Positive Psych Club Teams
summary: This purpose of this club is to teach different aspects of positive psychology - the science behind happiness and how to implement the information into your own life to help you live the good life!

name: DUX Presents
description: The concept of the organization itself is simple: screen classic, critically acclaimed, art, and cult films. Films will be briefly introduced, and then shown on a big screen. Following the viewing, anyone interested in discussing the film is free to stick around for a few minutes to do so. Ducks Present offers films through a critical lens of film viewership and creation; it is our aim to deliver a varied selection of genres, directors, eras, subject matters, moments in film history, and world perspectives, with the purpose of connecting the film-enjoying community and increasing film literacy and understanding campus wide.
summary: Ducks Present is a film-screening club on campus for critically acclaimed/cult films, showing curated works with the common goal of looking at film with a critical, filmmaking lens, along with connecting campus through the medium of film.

name: Operation Smile University of Oregon
description: Non-profit student organization at the University of Oregon. Spreading awareness & raising money for safe cleft lip and cleft palate surgeries around the world. Educating students about the condition, community service, and fundraising are major objectives of the club.
summary: The organization's goal is to spread cleft lip/palate awareness, perform local community service, and give students the opportunity to travel and help communities in need around the world. Fundraising is also a major objective of the club.

name: Federalist Society
description:
The Federalist Society is founded on the principles that the state exists to preserve freedom, that the separation of governmental powers is central to our Constitution, and that it is emphatically the province and duty of the judiciary to say what the law is, not what it should be.

The Federalist Society seeks both to promote an awareness of these principles and to further their application through its activities. This entails reordering priorities within the legal system to place a premium on individual liberty, traditional values, and the rule of law. It also requires restoring the recognition of the importance of these norms among lawyers, judges, and law professors.

The Federalist Society's chief goal is to foster balanced and open debate about the fundamental principles of individual freedom and limited government, and about the idea that courts should say what the law is rather than what they wish it to be. Our members hold diverse and often conflicting views on a broad range of issues. Membership is open to anyone who wants to join.

We promote awareness of Federalist principles at the University of Oregon by organizing activities such as speeches and debates by noted legal scholars and intellectuals.
summary: FedSoc's chief goal is to foster balanced and open debate about the fundamental principles of individual freedom and limited government. Our members hold diverse and often conflicting views on a broad range of issues.

name: Mu Phi Epsilon- Nu Chapter
description: Mu Phi Epsilon is a Co-Educational Professional Music Fraternity with more than 75,000 members incollegiate and alumni chapters around the world. The Nu Chapter at the University of Oregon has had along history at the School of Music and Dance and includes distinguished alumna MarAbel Frohnmayeramong its members. In keeping with the values of Mu Phi Epsilon, which is service through music, the NuChapter invests in utilizing music for outreach and service. This includes events within the School ofMusic and Dance (SOMD), all UO students, and within the Springfield/Eugene community. These eventsare meant to inspire community with lovers of music, volunteer our talents to perform or educate locals,and bring further professional development to students within out organization.
summary: Mu Phi Epsilon is a Co-Educational Professional Music Fraternity with more than 75,000 members incollegiate and alumni chapters around the world.

name: Taking Up Space
description: Taking Up Space is an open invitation to all creatives who want to connect and collaborate with other people of color. We prepare members for the industry ahead through passion projects and career development, while also supporting a safe space for BIPOC students.
summary: Taking Up Space is an open invitation to all creatives who want to connect and collaborate with other people of color. We prepare members for the industry ahead through passion projects and career development while also supporting a safe space for BIPOC.

name: UO Prison Education Program
description:
The Prison Education Program was established in 2016, building on over 10 years of collaboration between the University of Oregon and the Department of Corrections to offer credited classes through the Inside-Out Program.

In addition to offering upper-division classes, we also have a variety of not-for-credit activities, including:

  • Book discussions, including bilingual discussions conducted in Spanish and English

  • Academic workshops, including academic writing and communication skills 

  • Lectures by UO faculty on a wide variety of subjects 

  • Coordinated engagement between clubs at OSP and corresponding clubs at the UO

  • An on-campus exhibition of inmate artwork donated to the program (Spring 2019) 

  • Book donations to multiple prisons, including special requests from staff at the prisons for particular subject areas and Spanish language materials

We work in an interdisciplinary academic framework, and with a broad range of students, campus organizations, community groups, and national organizations working to improve educational opportunities in prisons.

The Prison Education Program also works with UO students through an internship and "alumni" program. Students are involved in assisting with classes, running a discussion group at a juvenile facility, holding outreach events with at-risk youth, and running book drives to support prison education.
summary: UO Prison Education Program

name: Amnesty International- University of Oregon Chapter
description:
Amnesty Internationalʼs vision is a world in which every person enjoys all of the human rights enshrined
in the Universal Declaration of Human Rights and other international human rights instruments. In
pursuit of this vision, Amnesty Internationalʼs mission is to undertake research and action focused on
preventing and ending grave abuses of these rights. Amnesty International is independent of any
political ideology, economic interest, or religion. The Amnesty International Chapter at the University of
Oregon, while continuing within the mission of Amnesty International, will work to engage students in
the University with global human rights issues. We will work to educate one another and the larger
community about various issues as they relate to human rights, and we will participate and engage in
activism to promote the protection and acquisition of rights, and to prevent further and continued
abuses of human rights.
summary: We meet every other Thursday from 6-7 p.m. beginning Week 2 in Condon 201 for Spring Term. Check out our Instagram @aisoregon for additional meeting information.

name: Crochet Club

Hi Everyone!! Come and crochet with us!

encouraging artistic freedom within the art of crochet and other fiber arts.
