name: K-Pop Club at UO
description: A nice place to meet fellow K-Pop fans and talk about your favorite groups. We watch music videos and hold special events. Some of those events include Photocard trading and decorating, Kpop jeopardy, bracket rankings, and may more. We listen to our members about their favorite groups while collaborating on themes of the week for songs. Follow us @kpopclub_UO on Instagram to see more and meeting updates!
summary: A nice place to meet fellow K-Pop fans and talk about your favorite groups. We mostly watch music videos and hold special events. Follow us @kpopclub_UO on Instagram to see more and meeting updates!

name: Neurodiversity Alliance at UO
description:
The goals of the Neurodiversity Alliance are to make a space where neurodivergent students can connect with each other, work together to advocate for the success of neurodivergent students at UO, and mentor each other through getting connected with resources, accomplishing our academic and career goals, and brainstorming ideas that can help make college easier for diverse minds. 

 

We currently meet weekly with a discussion topic relevant to college success as neurodivergent people, such as getting help through the AEC, study tips, or managing having roommates. We also have events to socialize with each other (like fidget toy swaps) and hold events to educate on and advocate for neurodivergent issues on campus. 

The club is run entirely by neurodivergent students. All the current campus resources that exist for us are helpful but are run by neurotypical people. We believe that neurodivergent leadership and mentoring from neurodivergent grad students and upperclassmen could make a big difference to the success of neurodivergent students at UO. Neurodivergent students are statistically more likely to drop out of college because they don't get the help that they need, and these issues disproportionately affect neurodivergent gender minorities and people of color who are less likely to be able to get a diagnosis or services. The Neurodiversity Alliance gives us a place to work together and advocate for each other in community and know that we are not alone. 

 

Spring 2026 Meeting Info: Thursdays from 5:30-6:30PM (PLC 072)

Join Our Discord Here (or by clicking the Earth icon below!): https://discord.gg/Wmjy6xpTmk
summary: A space where neurodivergent students can connect and work together to mentor each other and brainstorm ideas to be successful in our academic and career goals, advocate for neurodivergent students at UO, and connect with resources.

name: UpBeat UO Tap Club
description: We are an all-levels club aiming to create a fun, light-hearted space where UO students can create, practice, and learn tap dancing. This year we will be doing workshops, improv, guest choreography, and possibly a few performances! We will have a couple meetings during Fall 2025, and will get into a more regular schedule Winter and Spring 2025-26. Check us out on Instagram for the latest updates! @upbeatUOtap
summary: Come tap dance with us and a community of all-levels UO tap dancers! Latest updates can be found on Instagram @upbeatUO.

name: National Association of Black Journalists
description:
Collective of BIPOC students who produce content and collaborate on portfolio work centering around social justice, entertainment and culture. We are a safe space for multimedia projects that reflect our student body's diverse upbringings. 

Any small creative idea is possible with the help of community and other creative minds in the club.
summary: Collective of BIPOC students who produce content and collaborate on portfolio work centering around social justice, entertainment and culture. We are a safe space for multimedia projects that reflect our student body's diverse upbringings.

name: WE the Ensemble
description: The organization serves to promote and be an advocate for music education, particularly for people with disability and/or those from underserved communities. Our organization aims to spread awareness of accessibility and the use of music in special education and in community settings. We provide a space where all, regardless of ability, are welcomed and encouraged to explore their musical talents. Our end-of-year showcase is a public performance event featuring an ensemble of our members, who perform for a small audience.
summary: We the Ensemble is a club committed to teaching students with disability music education and instrument literacy by offering fun, interactive activities and hands-on experimentation with different musical instruments.

name: Artificial Intelligence Student Association
description:
AISA is an inclusive collective aimed at demystifying Artificial Intelligence (AI) for students from all academic domains. Our ethos is rooted in the belief that AI is not a distant future technology, but a versatile and essential resource at our fingertips TODAY. As a foundational entity, we are laying the groundwork for a culture of innovation, interdisciplinary collaboration, and creative application of AI. 

AI is something every student should know how to use, build with, and think critically about.


Through speaker events, hands-on workshops, collaborative projects, and industry site visits, we’re building a culture of experimentation, creativity, and real-world impact.

Everyone is welcome to attend our weekly meetings on Monday @ 6-7pm, check our website for room & RSVP details. If you're interested in AI, JOIN AISA!
summary: We welcome students from all backgrounds to join AISA, as we believe that interdisciplinary collaboration is critical to understanding the full potential of AI. If you are interested in learning more about AI and its impact on your field, we welcome you.

name: Oregon Law Technology Club
description: The Oregon Law & Technology Society is steadfast in its mission to sculpt a vanguard of legal professionals, harmoniously merging legal acumen with technological prowess. In line with ABA Rule 1.1, Comment 8, we are devoted to equipping UO Law students with not only the theoretical knowledge but also the hands-on skills essential for the modern legal sphere. Our dedication extends from fostering a profound understanding of the transformative power of AI in legal research, the intricacies of e-discovery processes, and the efficiency of contract automation, to the ethical considerations and cybersecurity challenges posed by emerging tech. We place particular emphasis on elevating students' capabilities in advanced data management, FinTech intersections, and the practical application of tools ranging from Excel to AI-powered legal solutions. At the nexus of law and technology, our goal is to champion UO Law students as they lead, innovate, and redefine the boundaries of tomorrow's legal frontier.
summary: Equipping law students with tech proficiency in AI, e-discovery, and contract automation. Advancing innovation at the nexus of law, cybersecurity, FinTech, and data management

name: American Mathematical Society (UO Chapter)
description:
The mission of the American Mathematical Society (AMS) is: To further the interests of mathematical research, scholarship, and education, serving the national and international community through publications, meetings, advocacy, and other programs.

 

The goals of the AMS are as follows:

  1. Promote mathematical research, its communication and uses
  2. Encourage and promote the transmission of mathematical understanding and skills
  3. Support mathematical education at all levels
  4. Advance the status of the profession of mathematics, encouraging and facilitating full participation of all individuals
  5. Foster an awareness and appreciation of mathematics and its connections to other disciplines and everyday life

 

The purpose of the AMS Graduate Student Chapter Program is to make meaningful contributions to the professional development of graduate students in the mathematical sciences and to encourage graduate students to engage with the Society. The AMS reserves the right to limit the total number of approved AMS Chapters. The AMS will evaluate this threshold as needed and announce to the community when/if additional chapters will be placed on hold. 

 

The AMS Graduate Student Chapter objectives and purpose shall be consistent with the mission and goals of the AMS.
summary: The mission of the American Mathematical Society (AMS) is: To further the interests of mathematical research, scholarship, and education, serving the national and international community through publications, meetings, advocacy, and other programs.

name: Omega Psi Phi
description:
Omega Psi Phi Fraternity, Inc. is the first international fraternal organization founded on the campus of a historically black college.

On the evening of November 17, 1911, Omega Psi Phi was founded inside the Science Building (later renamed Thirkield Hall) at Howard University located in Washington, D.C. The founders were three undergraduates — Edgar Amos Love, Oscar James Cooper and Frank Coleman. Joining them was their faculty adviser, Ernest Everett Just.

From the initials of the Greek phrase meaning, “friendship is essential to the soul“, the name Omega Psi Phi was derived. That phrase was selected as the motto.

Manhood, Scholarship, Perseverance and Uplift were adopted as Cardinal Principles.
summary: Omega Psi Phi Fraternity, Inc. is the first international fraternal organization founded on the campus of a historically black college.

name: National Pan-Hellenic Council
description:
Founded on May 10, 1930, the National Pan-Hellenic Council (NPHC) is the organization home to nine historically Black, Greek lettered organizations.

The National Purpose of the NPHC is to promote unity and expose members to the “service for life” philosophy and foster leadership development and scholarship.

Our Purpose as the NPHC at the UO is to cultivate and encourage high scholastic and ethical standards, develop leaders and promote the brotherhood and sisterhood in which all of our organization’s purposes entail, foster ideas of service and charity with a primary focus on our Black community and the Eugene community we serve, create an environment that respects the dignity and worth of every individual within the council, and continuously stimulate the ambition of council members in order to influence and carry out our purposes within everything we do.
summary: The National Pan-Hellenic Council (NPHC) is a collaborative umbrella council composed of historically African American fraternities and sororities

name: Multicultural Sorority and Fraternity Council
description: MSFC is home to many UO students. As a community, the chapters apart of MSFC seek to promote the general welfare and support the diversity of all students within the FSL community and beyond. Founded upon the principles of culture and creating opportunity for its members, MSFC organizations offer an experience that allows for the free exchange of ideas, programs, and services; promotes awareness of cultural diversity, and empowers students to use their voices to foster positive social change on campus.
summary: The Multicultural Sorority and Fraternity Council (MSFC) is the governing and representative body for 3 multicultural organizations at UO.

name: Kappa Alpha Psi Fraternity, Inc.
description: Kappa Alpha Psi Fraternity, Inc. (ΚΑΨ) is a historically African American fraternity. Since the fraternity's founding on January 5, 1911, at Indiana University Bloomington, it has never restricted membership based on color, creed, or national origin though membership traditionally is dominated by black men. The fraternity has over 260,000 members with 721 undergraduate and alumni chapters in every state of the United States, and international chapters in ten countries.
summary: Kappa Alpha Psi Fraternity, Inc. (ΚΑΨ) is a historically African American fraternity. Since the fraternity's founding on January 5, 1911 at Indiana University Bloomington.

name: Student Advocacy and Action for Environmental Justice
summary:
SAAEJ is a law school student organization committed to environmentalism embodying all forms of
justice and intentionally opposed to injustice.

name: Labor and Employment Law Association (fka Labor and Employment Relations Association)
description: The mission of the Labor and Employment Law Association is to foster communication and innovation among workers, management, and neutrals; educate and train the local community about labor and employment practices and rights; and affirm fundamental worker and human rights in the workplace. LELA encourages research in all aspects of the fields of labor, employment, and the workplace. We help graduate students build professional relationships now to help them succeed in their careers.
summary: LELA's mission to include many perspectives of labor and employment relations, contributes to the ASUO mission by bringing in differing viewpoints.

name: Students for Justice and Human Rights at the University of Oregon
summary: Our group is committed to creating a more just and equitable community. We hope to build a community founded on leadership, courage, nonviolence, and activism.

name: Philosophical Society at the UO
description: The UO Philosophical Society aims to offer students ample opportunity to share questions, concerns, and ideas. It is our goal to provide an environment where students of all majors, levels of expertise, and backgrounds can come together to participate in philosophical discourse.
summary: The UO Philosophical Society aims to offer students ample opportunity to share questions, concerns, and ideas. It is our goal to provide an environment where students of all majors, levels of expertise, and backgrounds can come together to participate.

name: Students for the Future of the Urban Farm
description: The primary mission of the Students for the Future of the Urban Farm is to create space for the community of students on this campus who are committed to the continued establishment and growth of the university's Urban Farm. This community includes, but is certainly not limited to farm volunteers and students with interests in landscape architecture, food studies, agriculture, planning, climate justice and community organizing. In the face of the Urban Farm's expansion to its second location, as well as the reduction of the current farm space in the face of construction of Phase II of the Knight Campus, the inclusion of student voices in the decisions made on behalf of the farm is especially critical. In order to ensure there is continued engagement of the student body in these matters, a core student organization is needed to continue to spearhead these efforts. The centering of student voices and support for urban farm students as the farm continues to evolve over the next decade is another key mission of this organization.
summary: The primary mission of the Students for the Future of the Urban Farm is to create space for the community of students on this campus who are committed to the continued establishment and growth of the university's Urban Farm.

name: Estate Planning and Probate Law Forum
summary: The goal of EPPLF is to provide an opportunity for students who are interested in estate planning and probate to learn about practicing in the area. EPPLF will invite estate planning and probate attorneys to speak at seminars where students can ask Q's.

name: University of Oregon Media Studies Club
description:
Media Studies Club is a group for people who are excited about media. If you're excited about a magazine, a podcast, a music video, or any other media text, we welcome you to our discussions and activities. 

This club is open to all majors. During the Winter term of 2025-2026, MSC is meeting . Please see our instagram and twitter accounts (both @uo_media) for meeting announcements and other updates.
summary: Media Studies Club is a group for people who are excited about media.

name: Oregon Software Consulting
description: Our mission is to empower students to apply their software, business, and technology development skills to solve real-world problems for organizations, businesses, and individuals that they may face. We are dedicated to engaging students to innovate and deliver customized solutions. Oregon Software Consulting is devoted to valuable hands-on experience, networking opportunities, and helping students achieve their goals.
summary: Oregon Software Consulting is a student-run organization that engages students to apply and develop their software, business, and technology development skills to solve real-world problems. We are committed to creating a fun community for students.

name: Drawing and Graphic Arts Club @ UO
description:
The Drawing & Graphic Arts Club @ UO is a student group engaged in the practice and enjoyment of the Graphic Arts, being defined as drawing, painting, printmaking, photography, & 2D graphics design. The goal of the organization is to provide a space for students to collaborate and practice visual arts together. Drawing is separated from the other graphic arts in the club name because it is the most common and the least setup-intensive practice, making it ideal for a group environment. That being said, all graphic arts are welcome and encouraged!

All skill levels and majors are welcome to join! 

We meet from 12-3pm every Friday and Sunday during school. Specific locations vary, and announced weekly on our Discord server!

Join our discord server!: https://discord.gg/Hatkn65gbZ
summary: The Drawing & Graphic Arts Club @ UO is a student group engaged in the practice and enjoyment of the Graphic Arts, being defined as drawing, painting, printmaking, photography, & 2D graphics design.

name: The University of Oregon Data Science Club
description:
The University of Oregon Data Science Club is a community that promotes the advancement of data science and its applications among University of Oregon students. We aim to foster a diverse community of individuals who are passionate about data science and provide educational and professional development opportunities to members through workshops, guest lectures, and other events. We also encourage members to work on data-driven projects and promote the use of data science for social good and ethical practices, while establishing a network of connections with professionals in the data science industry.

Join our discord!
summary: Passionate about data science? Join UODSCI! We're a community of passionate students advancing data science through projects, competitions, and educational opportunities, while promoting ethics and social good.

name: Alliance for Diversity in Science and Engineering
description:
The Alliance for Diversity in Science and Engineering is a nationally recognized undergraduate and graduate student organization at the University of Oregon that serves to provide professional development opportunities for underrepresented minorities in STEM and allies as well as to disseminate scientific knowledge to the greater Oregon community.  The following are a few events that ADSE has hosted in the past and will host in the future:

  • Diversity Lecture Series:  A series of STEM professional speakers from underrepresented backgrounds that talk about their work (research, job, etc.) followed by another presentation on their work to increase and support diversity, equity, inclusion efforts at their current and past institutions. 

  • Young Researchers Conference: An annual conference between all national chapters hosted by one chapter per year that welcomes all undergraduate student to present their research findings. 

  • Outreach: We strive to connect with the greater Oregon community by exposing URM communities to science. We are currently planning a community college event in collaboration with the University of Oregon's Undergraduate Research Symposium. We are also in the midst of reaching out to the Eugene Science Center to find volunteer opportunities to perform there. 

  • Science Communication Workshop Series: In collaboration with The Center for Science Communications Research through the School of Journalism, we host a series of workshops related to science communication for the public to attend and learn from.
summary: Our goals are directed towards developing a formal professional and social network for science students at the UO and to provide a platform for underrepresented minorities in STEM (e.g. people of color, women, LGBTQIA+, those with disabilities)

name: Folklore Society at UO
description: The UO Folklore Society supports folklore and ethnomusicology students, faculty and friends in their personal and professional endeavors. We seek to elevate diverse cultural research, practices, performances and traditional knowledge.
summary: The UO Folklore Society supports folklore and ethnomusicology students, faculty and friends in their personal and professional endeavors. We seek to elevate diverse cultural research, practices, performances and traditional knowledge.

name: Ducks on the Air Amateur Radio Club
description:
Amateur radio (often called ham radio) is the use of radios for educational and social purposes. Amateur radio is often used for emergency communications and contesting (on air competition). Ducks on the Air participates in these activities throughout the entire year, and engages in events with other universities such as Oregon State University and Portland State University.

 

Join our Discord Server! https://discord.gg/bQ2YHmV98j
summary: Amateur radio club that promotes, advocates for, and fosters international and domestic communications, emergency communications, and community events. All majors are encouraged to join! (You don't need to have your license yet!)

name: Women in Law
description: Undergraduate club focusing on women in legal studies and law with the purpose of empowering and assisting female undergraduates who wish to pursue a career in the legal industry, as well as anyone who is willing to learn. Our objective is to build a community where people can network, share their experiences, and acquire the skills and knowledge necessary to thrive in their future education as well as employment.
summary: Undergraduate club focusing on women in legal studies and law with the purpose of empowering and assisting female undergraduates who wish to pursue a career in the legal industry, as well as anyone who is willing to learn.

name: Letters of Love
description:
How Letters of Love Began

A message from our Founder & President, Grace Berbig

Hello! My name is Grace Berbig, I am the Founder & President of Letters of Love- an International 501(c)3 Non-Profit organization that provides emotional support for patients in Children’s Hospitals across the world.

 

First of all, I want to thank you (yes you!) for supporting my organization. Letters of Love means the absolute world to me, and I cannot explain how deeply grateful I am for all of the support we have received, and for each wonderful person that is a part of our growing family. Thank you for being a part of the team who fights to put smiles on the faces of children who are fighting a battle that no kid ever should.

 

Lots of people ask what inspired me to start Letters of Love. My answer, in short, is my mom. She is, and forever will be, to thank for every accomplishment Letters of Love has made and will continue making. When I was about eight years old, my mom was diagnosed with leukemia, a cancer located in the blood. Of course at such a young age I had no idea what cancer was, or why my mom was suddenly living in the hospital.

 

I did know, however, that I was going to make sure she knew that even though my family and I couldn’t be there with her, we were always thinking of her, supporting her, and loving her every second of her journey. Almost every day, my two little sisters and I would make piles of cards to plaster on my moms bland hospital walls, filling them with love and support.

 

She always loved those notes and cards more than anything, and my sisters and I loved making them. When I was ten, my beautiful, strong, brave mother lost her battle with cancer. After she passed away, I vowed to myself that I would devote my life to helping people who were fighting battles similar to my mom. I was inspired to create good and help people who are going through something similar as my mom did, and it has become my dream and passion to give back and change the world with kindness. 

​

My junior year of high school, I decided I wanted there to be a way for everyone to be able to make a difference without having to worry about their personal financial situation; a way for young people to help without having to worry about money. In October of 2018, I got the idea to start a club where students have the opportunity to emotionally support children battling cancer and other serious illnesses. Students could come and make cards, tie-blankets, friendship bracelets, and other support items for patients at the Children’s Hospital of Minnesota.

 

I officially began Letters of Love as a club in October 2018 at Orono High School. Since then, we have grown more than I ever could have imagined. We now donate to 30+ hospitals, and have over 68 high schools and 55 colleges across the nation actively participating in Letters of Love. We have received cards from over 17 different countries.

​

There are thousands of Letters of Love members across the world, spreading positivity and kindness one card and a time. I am so proud to say that Letters of Love has had the honor of providing emotional support to over 175,000 patients in Children’s Hospitals around the world. I am so thrilled to see how far this organization goes, as we continue to work towards our goal of providing emotional support for each and every patient in every Children’s Hospital across the world.
summary: Letters of Love is a growing nonprofit organization across U.S. universities with the sole purpose to spread kindness. LOL holds monthly meetings where attendees will make "letters of love" that will then be sent to kids in children's hospitals.

name: Ace of Ducks: Card Games at the University of Oregon
description: Our organization will teach students how to play poker and other card games. We will teach people about the strategies and how the skill can apply to real life. This includes decision-making and keeping calm under pressure, a fun way to learn life skills. We will host small tournaments where players can showcase their skills and win prizes. We do not play for cash prizes or cash games. Our meetings will include sessions to teach beginners and tournament-style poker games, including different skill groups. We play weekly on Thursdays at 6:15pm in HEDCO 220. Come by!
summary: Our organization will teach students how to play poker and other card games. We will teach people about the strategies and how the skill can apply to real life. This includes decision-making and keeping calm under pressure, a fun way to learn life skills

name: Queer Book Club
description: Once a month members meet to discuss a chosen book and vote on the next book from a list of member submissions. Members are not required to be queer themselves but every book read must/will include some aspect or theme of LGBTQIA+ experience.
summary: Read queer books!

name: Women In Economics Club
description:
The UO Women in Economics Club (WiE) was established in 2023 to support and meet the unique needs of women and gender-diverse individuals in the field of economics. WiE strives to build community, empower, and increase participation in economics through academic and social events. The club hosts guest speakers, roundtable discussions, professional development workshops, and more. Students undergraduate through PhD are welcome. 

Meetings: Every Monday from 6-7pm in Gerlinger 248. Hope to see you there!

https://linktr.ee/UO_Women_in_Economics
summary: A community to empower and encourage participation of women interested in Economics

name: MEDLIFE
description: The MEDLIFE Movement is a mission to empower the underprivileged in their fight for access to healthcare, education and safe homes all around the world. Originating in the United States as a non-profit organization, MEDLIFE has grown internationally with partners in Latin America and Africa. Here, we listen, collaborate and innovate to provide communities in need with the right resources to thrive. Whether you wish to volunteer, participate in educational workshops, assist/engage in clinics, shadow medical professionals internationally or travel abroad with peers to aid in the betterment of communities, MEDLIFE is an incredible guidance resource. Through the active participation of our chapter members and collaborators, we can work together to cultivate change around the world!
summary: Official University of Oregon chapter of MEDLIFE International, a non-profit organization working to provide medicine, education and development for low-income families everywhere.

name: Rural Student Coalition (RSC)
description: 1. Mission: The Rural Student Coalition seeks to ensure the academic, mental, and physical well-being of students from rural backgrounds as well as establishing community and accentuating the importance of rural communities on the University of Oregon campus. 2. Goals: - Ensure rural students are well informed about resources meant to ease the transition into independent student life and perpetuate success beyond graduation - Ensure the needs of rural students are met at University of Oregon and the communities within - Ensure the recognition of values, knowledge, and backgrounds unique to rural students that are often overlooked by areas where urbanization and surplus resources are centered - Create community on UO’s campus amongst self-identified rural students - Provide a safe space for further marginalized rural students (based on physical and/or mental ability, sexual orientation, gender identity and presentation, race, ethnicity, nationality, parental and/or marital status, and any other personal identifiers that may make a student further susceptible to societal or systemic harm) - Educate non-rural students, faculty, and staff about rural communities and their international and domestic impacts.
summary: The Rural Student Coalition seeks to ensure the academic, mental, and physical well-being of students from rural backgrounds as well as establishing community and accentuating the importance of rural communities on the University of Oregon campus.

name: Bookstagram: Behind the Scenes
description:
In Bookstagram: Behind the Scenes, we will be looking into many of the books and genres that have become popular on social media platforms such as Instagram and TikTok. We want to be a place where readers and bookworms of all kinds can come together and step away from the screens, and form connections over a love of literature in the real world. It will be a place where people can discuss their favorite books, share love or hatred for fictional characters and worlds, or review recent/popular reads. We aim to bring the comfort of reading on a cozy autumn afternoon and combine it with the creativity, passion, and connective powers of social media.

Meeting times for the 25'-26' school year: 

Tuesdays @ 6 pm in the Coquille Room 

We hope to see you with a book in hand at our next meeting!
summary: BBTS is a nontraditional book club that will discuss books and bookish topics that have become popular in social media. We'll talk about favorite genres, host bookish game nights, spread positivity for reading and the reader community, and so much more!

name: Hindu YUVA @ UO
description: The mission of Hindu YUVA at UO is to bring together both Hindu and non-Hindu students to raise awareness about what Hinduism is. As one of the largest and oldest religions in the world, we believe it is necessary that there be an organization on campus to represent the interests of the Hindu community present here. We hope to create a comfortable atmosphere where students can come and celebrate the Hindu culture. We hope to achieve these goals by having team-building games, yoga and meditation, lively and exciting debates, and presentations at our weekly meetings. All in all, we want to build a small Hindu community within UO so that students can come and discover a home away from home.
summary: Create a safe space for students on campus that want to learn about Hindu culture or celebrate Hindu culture on campus.

name: Journalism and Communication Graduate Student Association
description: The Journalism and Communication Graduate Student Association (JCGSA) is a dedicated body committed to fostering a collaborative and supportive community for graduate students pursuing studies in journalism and communication. Serving as a platform for networking, professional development, and academic enrichment, the JCGSA actively engages its members in dialogues, workshops, and events that advance research and practice in the fields of journalism and communication.
summary: The Journalism and Communication Graduate Student Association (JCGSA) is a dedicated body committed to fostering a collaborative and supportive community for graduate students pursuing studies in journalism and communication.

name: Ducks with Diabetes
description: Ducks with Diabetes is a club dedicated to connecting all diabetic ducks by spreading awareness and building the diabetes community at UO. Ducks with Diabetes is the UO's chapter of The Diabetes Link, a nationwide organization dedicated to helping "teens and young adults navigate their diabetes among an ever-changing and fast-paced world." We meet up about twice a term to do anything from having craft or game nights, attending diabetes advocacy events in the community, or grabbing a bite to eat and catching up over pizza at Bartolotti's. You do not need to have diabetes to be a part of the club; all community members who want to learn more or show their support for the diabetes community are welcome! Please reach out via email at duckwithdiabetes@thediabeteslink.org if you have any questions. We'd love to see you at our next event!
summary: This club at the University of Oregon is dedicated to connecting diabetic ducks, spreading awareness & building community.

name: The Paintingclub
description:
The Painting club's goal is to connect student artists through painting together and studying principles of color, light, values, and other essential skills such as sketch, anatomy, and perspective/accuracy. Our vision is to have a save space where free sources are there to support artists ideas, ambitions, and projects. Future activities will include going on field trips to do open air paintings, painting public murals, and selling paintings to fundraise for the club. 

Bi-weekly meeting is required to be a valid member. We'll have weekly study sessions attendance is optional.
summary: The Paintingclub is a place where student artists can connect and find the message that they are trying to deliver through painting. At times you might loss the initial joy that motivated you to create. This is the place where you could find it again.

name: Puddles and Cups Tea Club
description:
Sip, quack, and steep with Puddles and Cups Tea Club! If you are a tea-enthusiast this is the club for you! Come hang out, drink some tea, and meet other fellow tea lovers.

 

Meetings every Monday from 6:30-7:30 in EMU Rogue Room, 140

Follow us on Instagram - uo_puddlesandcups
summary: Sip, quack, and steep with Puddles and Cups Tea Club! If you are a tea-enthusiast, this is the club for you! Come hang out, drink some tea, and meet other fellow tea lovers.

name: Affinity Organization Coalition
description: The purpose of the Affinity Organization Coalition is strategic advocacy on behalf of the University of Oregon School of Law’s minority community. The Coalition also exists to provide a forum for dialogue and action on topics and issues that impact the School’s minority community. In support of the Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion Action Plan (DAP), the Coalition exists to provide the School Administrators with information and advice regarding issues that impact minority faculty, staff and employees. This includes the execution and implementation of a law journal that promotes these ideals.
summary: The purpose of the Affinity Organization Coalition is strategic advocacy on behalf of the University of Oregon School of Law’s minority community and to also provide a forum for those affected in this community.

name: Friends of MSF/Doctors Without Borders Student Chapter University of Oregon
description: Doctors Without Borders / Médecins Sans Frontières (MSF) is an international, independent, medical humanitarian organization that delivers emergency aid to people affected by armed conflict, epidemics, natural disasters and exclusion from healthcare. MSF offers assistance to people based on need, irrespective of race, religion, gender or political affiliation. MSF is a non-profit, self-governed organization. Today, MSF is a worldwide movement of 24 associations, bound together as MSF International, based in Switzerland. Thousands of health professionals, logistical and administrative staff – most of whom are hired locally – work on programs in more than 60 countries. Friends of Médecins Sans Frontières/Doctors Without Borders is a voluntary student led society focused on supporting MSF and raising awareness for their mission and global impact. Winter 2026 Meetings are even Mondays at 5:30pm located in Mackenzie Hall 240A (first meeting is week 2)!
summary: Friends of Médecins Sans Frontières/Doctors Without Borders (FoMSF) is a student led chapter at the UO focused on supporting MSF, a non-profit, international, NGO committed to providing free medical care to those in need regardless of race or politics.

name: Performing Arts Association for International Identities
description:
The Performing Arts Association for International Identities (PAI) is an organization with a mission to promote and encourage performing arts in students from diverse cultural backgrounds. The organization's ultimate goal is to help students develop their skills in the performing arts and showcase their love and interest in different cultures through various platforms.

 

PAI aims to provide a safe and nurturing space where students can learn and grow together. The organization's focus is on fostering a positive relationship between oneself and the art forms of different cultures. PAI's bylaws state that the organization is committed to creating an environment that encourages people to express themselves through performing arts.

 

PAI believes that performing arts can be used as a tool to promote intercultural understanding and promote peace among communities. The organization provides a variety of programs and initiatives to achieve this goal. Some of these programs include workshops, seminars, and cultural events that bring together students from different cultures and backgrounds.

 

In summary, PAI's mission is to provide a platform for students to showcase their love and interest in different cultures through performing arts. The organization aims to provide a safe and supportive environment for students to learn and grow together while fostering a positive relationship with oneself and with the art forms of different cultures.
summary: PAI at the UO focuses on creating an association for students interested in or who associate themselves with any cultural background to provide a space for people from all backgrounds to foster the performing arts side of different cultural backgrounds.

name: Ghanaian Students at University of Oregon
description:
We are a vibrant community of students with Ghanaian heritage or a deep connection to Ghana, united by a shared appreciation for the culture and traditions of the motherland. Whether you have roots in Ghana, are married to a Ghanaian, have parents from Ghana, or simply want to be part of the Ghanaian community, you are welcome here! Our community is open to students and the broader University of Oregon community who are eager to experience and celebrate Ghanaian culture.

Our mission is to celebrate the richness of Ghanaian and African culture while fostering meaningful connections across diverse backgrounds. Guided by our values of unity, excellence, and cultural celebration, we create an inclusive space where everyone can thrive.
summary: We strive  to create a second home for Ghanaian students on campus, where they can thrive together in their educational journey , social life, spiritual wellbeing and facilitate cross-cultural exchange with mutual respect

name: East African Student Association
description: EASA is a club focused on creating events and a tight knit community for East African students at the University of Oregon. The club strives to be a welcoming environment and a safe space for all.
summary: EASA is a club focused on creating events and a tight knit community for East African students at the University of Oregon. The club strives to be a welcoming environment and a safe space.

name: Music History Club
description:
Music History Club's goal is to get fellow students into the art of music. It's a place for music lovers to meet other music lovers and discuss, review, and learn about albums that are considered classics. Besides simply reviewing the album and deeming it good or bad, we will look at all aspects of the album; why it was created, what was going on in the world when it was made, and the artist(s) behind the album itself. Each week we focus on a different album, usually something from the 1960-2010s. We give a brief history of the album, then have a guided discussion about it where anyone can voice their thoughts.

We are focused on providing a detailed and engaging perspective on the history of these albums, with fun discussions and interactive activities related to the album/artist.
summary: Music History Club is a place for students to learn about the history, artistry, and cultural significance of music. Additionally, it's a place for music lovers and people who want to get more into music to bond over the art form.

name: Beta Alpha Psi Beta Chapter
description:
Beta Alpha Psi is an honor organization for Accounting, Finance, and Data Analytics students and professionals. Our mission is to promote growth in our members through our efforts in developing professional skills and being involved in our community.

 

Each Thursday, Beta Alpha Psi holds a weekly speaker meeting where accounting professionals from various industries share their expertise, insights, and career experiences with members. These meetings provide invaluable opportunities for students to learn about different career paths, industry trends, and best practices directly from seasoned professionals.

 

In addition to speaker meetings, Beta Alpha Psi organizes networking and recruiting events to connect its members with potential employers, like Meet the Firms, Meet the Professionals, and various firm site visits. Furthermore, Beta Alpha Psi is committed to giving back to the community through volunteer events. Whether it's organizing book drives, volunteering for GrassRoots Gardens or the Eugene Marathon, or offering free tax services for the local community, members of Beta Alpha Psi contribute their time and skills to make a positive impact in the world.

 

If you’re interested in joining, feel free to come to one of our Thursday meetings or reach out on our Engage page or website!

 

UO BAP in the news! | https://www-klcc-org.cdn.ampproject.org/c/s/www.klcc.org/economy-business/2024-02-06/uo-students-offer-to-help-low-income-taxpayers-with-filing-process?_amp=true
summary: Beta Alpha Psi is an honor organization for Accounting, Finance, and Data Analytics students and professionals. Our mission is to promote growth in our members through our efforts in developing professional skills and being involved in our community.

name: UO Family & Human Services Association
description: The Family & Human Services Association (UOFHSA) is aims to create a community for those interested in human services at the University of Oregon. Events are geared towards FHS and related majors but open to anyone who wants to join. UOFHSA also provides an opportunity to gain information and network with local human service organizations.
summary: A community for Family & Human Services Majors and anyone interested in the human services field.

name: UO Nursing Association
description:
A welcoming club for anyone interested in pursuing a career in nursing starting at the University of Oregon! We want to give help to guide and inform new and already established students on how they can go about becoming nurses starting at UO. We will hold bi-monthly meetings to discuss nursing-related topics and help give any resources necessary to succeed in the many prerequisite classes. We will also talk about future steps in the field of nursing and how you can proceed with getting into colleges and graduate schools related to the field.

We hold meetings every other Wednesday starting week 2 from 6-7pm in room B042 of the Science Library.

Please note that our old email (uonursing@gmail.com) is currently defunct due to two-step verification struggles. For the time being, we will be using uonursingassn@gmail.com. Feel free to reach out to us if you have further questions!
summary: Our club will help people figure out how to get into a nursing school on a 2-3 year plan, and will give them all the information necessary to get accepted into programs across the country. We also will give academic support for any prerequisite classes.

name: Neuroscience Club
description: Our mission is to create a vibrant community for neuroscience majors. To empower minds through exploration, understanding, and collaboration. We aim to spark meaningful discussions, promote innovative research, and inspire lifelong learning within the community.  We believe in the power of partnership and aim to create a supportive environment where individuals can forge meaningful connections with peers, experts, and professionals in the field of neuroscience. Through engaging workshops, interactive seminars, and immersive activities, we facilitate experiential learning that transcends the confines of traditional education.
summary: The Neuroscience Club ignites curiosity and fosters understanding through captivating talks,interactive workshops, and thought-provoking discussions, unraveling the complexities of the human brain.To expand students passion for the field of neuroscience.

name: NMDP On Campus - University of Oregon
description:
Our mission is to engage students in the NMDP mission to save lives by:

Providing meaningful, unique opportunities for students to learn about blood cancers and other life-threatening diseases treated by a blood stem cell transplant.

Increasing awareness of the need for young, committed, racially and ethnically diverse registry members who can save a life.

Holding campus-wide education/awareness events, donor registry recruitment events, and activities to give the entire student body the opportunity to join in this global effort.

Learn More Here!

 

Key Words: Medical, Medicine, Disease, Stem Cells, Service, Do Good, Donation
summary: To educate about life-threatening diseases and blood cancers treated by blood stem cell transplants and recruit committed, diverse, young UO students to join the National Marrow Donor Program registry to provide hope and save lives.

name: iGEM UOregon
summary: The goal of the UOregon iGem team is to provide undergraduate students with an opportunity to perform research in synthetic biology while growing as independent researchers and practicing skills in networking, fundraising, education, and outreach.

name: Ducks 4 Israel
description: An inclusive community at the University of Oregon dedicated to celebrating the diverse culture and history of Israel.
summary:
Ducks 4 Israel is an opportunity for students to be involved and engaged in pro-Israel activism on
campus.

name: Pokemon at UO
summary:
The goal of this club is simple: Give the University of Oregon and Surrounding College students a way to
interact with other students who enjoy all things Pokémon.

name: Women in Psychology
description:
Women in Psychology aims to create a safe space for people with an interest in psychology to collaborate and support each other. Our goals include encouraging resources by hosting workshops on internship search, grad school preparation, study abroad help, and information for exploring other resources. We strive to help students build a network of valuable connections within their field to support each other through their college journey. 

We will be holding a minimum of two meetings monthly and plan to have psychology related events throughout each term.
summary: Women in Psychology aims to create a safe space for people with an interest in psychology to collaborate and support each other. We encourage resources on internship search, grad school preparation, study abroad help, and other support on your journey.

name: Portland Student Council at UO
description: Founded in January 2017, the Portland Student Council (PSC) at the University of Oregon Portland was established to address the need for organized student representation across 5 colleges and schools and 13 majors. In April 2024, the PSC gained recognition from the Associated Students of the University of Oregon (ASUO). Our mission is to empower all students by fostering social enrichment and professional development, enhancing campus life through leadership and inclusive events.
summary: We aim to empower all students by fostering social enrichment and professional development, enhancing campus life through leadership and inclusive events.

name: Civic Impact Coalition
description:
Welcome to Civic Impact Coalition!

 

Our club connects students interested in Public Affairs (planning, public policy, nonprofits, volunteering, civic action) with opportunities to serve the community and grow their professional skills. Whether it's a one-time volunteer event or our year-long project, we have something for you! Become a member and follow our events page for up-to-date information on volunteer events and activities. 

Follow us on Instagram at @civicimpactcoalition for updates, and contact May (mayms@uoregon.edu) to get added to our member channel on Teams. 

All are welcome! No experience required :)
summary: The mission of the club is to support interdisciplinary learning in public affairs, encourage dialogue within PPPM, facilitate real world practice in service to Oregon communities, and increase PPPM's visibility and long-term memory.

name: UO Men's Resource Center
summary: Men's Resource Center located in the EMU

name: The Coalition of Women’s Martial Arts
description:
The Coalition of Women’s Martial Arts, or the CWMA, is an organization meant to encourage and support both women and non-male identifying people in the martial arts community at the University of Oregon. We strive to create a welcoming community for everyone within the martial arts clubs at UO regardless of gender identity.

Our goal is to encourage more women and femme-identifying individuals to get involved in what has historically been a male-dominated sport, and to inspire confidence in them. We want people to find a sense of belonging within the CWMA while bonding and forming lasting relationships with other members. Furthermore, we want to foster a culture of respect to ensure that everyone feels safe within these spaces with members consisting of both those who are femme-identifying and those who are not, but believe in our goals.

Practice every Sunday from 1230pm-130pm in Mat room 47!!!!
summary: The CWMA is an organization meant to encourage and support non-male identifying individuals in the UO martial arts community. Our goal is to facilitate a safe and welcoming environment for everyone so that we may share and bond over our love of the sport

name: Manga discussion and creation club
description:
"Manga is a traditional Japanese sequential art form, and we’re here to provide a fun space where students can connect, create, share, and learn more about it. Whether you’re an experienced manga artist or just starting out, everyone is welcome in our club! We're also looking for individuals with creative ideas and stories to give them a platform to express their work in the form of manga. No need to be a pro artist—great ideas make great stories! We aim to help members gain valuable experience, whether for fun or even as a career. Feel free to reach out with any questions!"

Bi-Weekly workshop : Tuesday 6-8pm 

Weekly social meeting: Friday 6-8pm

#Manga #Comic #Social #story writing #Paneling #Japan #Culture #drawing #Art #Inking #Magazine
summary: Join us to explore and create manga! Whether you're a seasoned fan or totally new, everyone’s welcome. Share your ideas, learn the craft, and express your stories. No need for advanced art skills—your creativity is what counts!

name: Narcare UO
description: Narcare UO is a club that meets once a month for meetings and once a month for volunteering (with tabling throughout!) with a goal of addressing the opioid crisis throughout the community by focusing on naloxone distribution, harm reduction, and empowerment through education. The club meets monthly to discuss plans and equip peers with knowledge relevant to the community. Spreading awareness of naloxone use and harm reduction, the club aims to keep students and peers safe. Specific events include tabling, education sessions, and naloxone distribution sites. The club regularly communicates and connects with on-campus organizations to expand the network of impacts and widen student accessibility to naloxone. In addition, the club regularly volunteers around the community.
summary: Addressing the opioid crisis throughout the community by focusing on naloxone distribution, harm reduction, and empowerment through education.

name: The Rising Project
description: The Rising Project is a student-run creative studio dedicated to merging creativity with science communication. Through positive messaging, digestible and engaging media content, as well as easy solutions we aim to rise above the current intimidating and fearful media coverage surrounding the climate crisis. We want our fellow students and young community to feel that they can have a part in solving climate change right on campus all with just a few small adjustments to their routines and mentality.
summary: he Rising Project is a student-run creative studio dedicated to merging creativity with science communication

name: Jewish Voice for Peace at University of Oregon
description: Jewish Voice for Peace is a national, grassroots organization working towards Palestinian freedom and Judaism beyond Zionism. JVP is dedicated to fighting for Palestinian liberation and an end to the genocide in Gaza. Our chapter is committed to educating our community about the history of Palestine, history of Zionism, Judaism beyond Zionism, and what is happening in Palestine today. JVP’s core values are rooted in anti-imperialism, anti-racism, equality, and justice.
summary: Jewish Voice for Peace is a national, grassroots organization working towards Palestinian freedom and Judaism beyond Zionism.

name: Black Student Association
description:
The Black Student Association (BSA) is dedicated to fostering a vibrant community where individuals are empowered to achieve success through leadership and creativity. Grounded in our core values of Culture, Connection, Creativity, Progress, and Success, we strive to create an inclusive space where students of color can express themselves freely and grow both personally and professionally.

Our objectives are to cultivate a culturally rich environment that supports creativity, offers meaningful programming, and promotes education on industry best practices. By forming strong connections and driving engagement, we aim to provide the tools for students to excel. Our ultimate vision is to strengthen the representation and success of Black professionals, contributing to a more diverse and innovative future.

We meet and operate primarily out of the Portland Campus.
summary:
To cultivate a culturally rich community where students of color feel welcomed to
express themselves freely and creatively. Offer programming that forms connections,
engagement, and provides education on best practices that'll fuel success.

name: Hacky Sack at UO
description:
Come kick it with us and hack! We meet about once a week, weather permitting— all skills are welcome! Follow our instagram for updates and details @hackysackatuo 

WE ARE STOKED TO SEE YOU THERE!
summary: Hacky Sack Club is an inclusive and open-minded community dedicated to the sack! We aim to increase the  interest in hacky sacking on campus. Hacky sack club is entirely for fun; all skill levels are welcome, beginners encourage.

We invite any student to come code, create, and collaborate in an innovative space for 24 hours and connect with their peers, mentors, and industry professionals. name: Game Development Club UO

Join the Game Development Club and Level Up Your Creativity! Whether you’re an avid gamer, creator, or just curious about game development, we’re the perfect spot to jump into game making. Join our game jams, where you can team up to design and build your own games in a laid-back setting. Stop by a meeting to make your game visions a reality.

 

Discord

description: Blossom Empowerment Project is dedicated to addressing the need for menstrual and personal hygiene products in underserved communities throughout Lane County. Our mission is to provide University of Oregon students with the opportunity to build a community with those who share a commitment to giving back, while fostering a safe and inclusive space for women, queer individuals, and people of all identities. Through our efforts, we aim to deepen students' understanding of reproductive health and foster a sense of connection and belonging for individuals who share common values.
summary: The Blossom Empowerment Project provides menstrual and hygiene products to underserved Lane County communities. We foster an inclusive, supportive space for students to engage in service, raise awareness, and build connections around reproductive health.

name: Clark Honors College Book Club at the UO
description: This organization serves to provide a safe space for all UO and CHC students to come together in reading one book a term together. This will include regular discussion meetings and book related activities. We will provide a space for students to discuss their current read as well as snacks at regular meetings. Our goal is to allow for a space for like-minded readers to connect through the medium of literature and to foster meaningful discussions around important topics related to life and reading.

FBD at University of Oregon is a non-profit organization that exists for the betterment of students’ architectural education, training, and practice at the University, as well as the greater Eugene community. The club uses the talents of architecture students to radically impact the lives of people in their community through modest design and construction solutions, community service, education and sustainable practices.

Freedom by Design™ is the AIAS community service program, this design-build based club uses the talents of College of Design students to radically impact the lives of people in their community.

Meetings every Tuesday at 6:15pm in LA 278

name: Perfect Pair
description: Perfect Pair fosters one-on-one, intergenerational connections between older adults and college students. We strive to reinforce a mutual sense of community and purpose for our pairs: a goal that underlies our efforts to combat loneliness and improve overall health and wellbeing. We achieve this by ensuring that our pair connections are personalized: based on shared background and interests. Simultaneously, we encourage pairs to re-engage with their passions by offering individualized and accessible programming. We hope for older adults and students to feel that their life perspective and quality of life have been meaningfully enhanced by their experience in our program.
summary: Perfect Pair is a national non-profit organization that seeks to pair up older adults and college students to form meaningful connections.

name: Desi Ducks
description: We are a student organization dedicated to celebrating South Asian culture through the energetic dance forms of Bollywood and Bhangra. Our goal is to provide a welcoming and safe space for students of all backgrounds to learn, practice, and build community! We’re a co-ed club open to everyone — whether you’ve been dancing your whole life or you’re stepping onto the dance floor for the very first time. No experience needed, just bring your energy — everyone is welcome!
summary: A student organization dedicated to celebrating South Asian culture through the energetic dance forms of Bollywood and Bhangra. Our goal is to provide a welcoming space for students of all background to learn, practice, and build community

name: Oregon Law Students Aligned Against Ableism
summary: Oregon Law Students Aligned Against Ableism

name: Oregon Quant Group
description: Our mission is to bring together the best minds at the University of Oregon and develop their ability to thrive as professionals in the field of Quantitative finance as well as in fields adjacent. OQG's culture is built off the idea that students will grow rapidly in an environment that supports them in learning about what they are the most interested in. As a result we have developed a robust pathway for driven students to explore their interests based on their aspirations, whether this is Mathematics, Finance, Coding or the intersection of all three. The OQG is comprised of two teams. Our research subdivision specializes in developing cutting edge, data backed research on a wide array of alternative assets as well as trading strategies that are built to be deployed on the market. Our technical team specializes in the creation of back tested models that are also used for the execution of trades or for the identification of arbitrage opportunities. Our technical team will work on a wide array of projects of interest which can range from the development of machine learning models to predict Heating Oil (HO=F) prices from NASA satellite data.
summary: “To foster a collaborative learning environment where members gain real-world experience, develop essential skills, and build a lifelong network in math, finance, or coding.”

name: Girls Skate
description: Girls skate is a club to create space in the skate community for women and female presenting skaters to learn and improve. The skatepark can be an intimidating place for beginners, as well as feminine presenting people. Our club is dedicated to creating a supportive environment for progression, and establishing a community of skaters who show up for each other and cheer on everyone's growth on their skate journey. If you are interested in learning but have been too nervous in the past this is the place for you! All people, of all genders and skill levels are welcome to join. To apply go to @uogirlsskate on instagram and fill out the google form in bio, or email uogirlsskate@gmail.com to receive the google form link.
summary: Girls Skate is a skate club dedicated to cultivating a supportive community for girls and feminine presenting skateboarders to learn and progress in a male-dominated environment. Open to all skill/experience levels and all genders.

name: Students Impacted by Chronic Conditions
description: SICC aims to provide a space for students to connect with peers who are going through similar experiences managing a significant chronic health condition. Peer support can help students find community, connect with resources, and learn coping strategies and perspectives from other students.
summary: SICC is a student organization that provides peer support, resources, outreach and information for students managing chronic health conditions.

name: Eating Disorder Awareness and Support
description:
We believe that college is a critical transitional period during which students are faced with new, and sometimes challenging life experiences. When students are exposed to negative food and body talk during this period in their lives, it can create a harmful impact on their relationship with food and body. To address this issue, Eating Disorder Awareness and Support (EDAS) aims to expand eating disorder education, awareness, and resources on the University of Oregon campus with the goal of minimizing students’ risk of developing eating disorders.

 

EDAS is a student-run club devoted to creating and strengthening a network of resources for students struggling with disordered eating, body image issues, or any habits or behaviors that fall out of the definition of normal eating. We are working with multiple departments on campus to strengthen existing resources for students as well as develop new ones. We hope to create a culture and community at the UO that is supportive of strong and positive relationships with food and body.    

Our club is intended to provide resources to help guide students towards positive relationships with food and body. We provide resources and education for all people, regardless of whether you have struggled with disordered eating or body image issues or know someone who has. We all live in a society governed by pervasive food and body rules, myths, and narratives that are harmful. With that awareness in mind, we are ALL able to participate in conversations about food and body. 

Throughout this website, you will notice the term "disordered eating” reoccur. We use this term to encompass the spectrum of varying behaviors that signal negative and unbeneficial relationships with food and body. Anyone, regardless of whether or not they have experienced disordered eating, can have difficult days with food and body due to the negative messaging we receive in society.

One of the most important pillars of our club is our educational efforts. EDAS offers presentations for organizations, clubs, classes, and communities on and off campus. EDAS offers two presentations, both geared towards initiating conversations around how to foster positive food and body relationships, and how societal factors may serve as a barrier to this goal. EDAS takes a preventative educational approach, meaning that educating individuals on building positive relationships with food and body empowers them to deflect negative messaging they hear in society and create a community in supporting one another.  

Our first presentation discusses topics such as the definition of normal eating, diet culture, body diversity, Health At Every Size, exercise and movement, how to support a loved one with an ED, and resources at the UO and Eugene area. Our second presentation focuses on an in-depth discussion about body positivity and body neutrality, and how these movements function within our society to support individuals in finding peace, appreciation, and love for their bodies. Neither of our presentations defines eating disorders, their subsets, behaviors, stories, numbers, sizes, or anything that may directly trigger an individual. 

We are able to accommodate our presentation style to best fit the audience at hand and are committed to being transparent and open about the topics we discuss. If you are interested, please feel free to contact us to discuss more of what our presentation looks like. Our presentations can be requested by contacting us at edas@uoregon.edu, or you can reach out to us using the contact form on our website's home page.

EDAS collaborates with the Collegiate Recovery Center (CRC) to host peer-led eating disorder support groups. Whether you're seeking a safe space to share your journey, engage in discussions about societal pressures related to food and body, or looking to support a loved one, our group is here for you. These groups are hosted from 6:30 to 7:30pm on Tuesdays in the CRC (EMU Room 331). We post updates about groups weekly on our Instagram!

Our mission is to foster a sense of community and empowerment by encouraging students to practice vulnerability with others. We strive to create an environment in which students can build confidence in their recovery and learn that they are not alone in their struggles. EDAS is committed to ensuring that every student feels heard, understood, and supported, as we believe in the transformative power of peer connections and shared experiences. Together, we can navigate the challenges, celebrate the victories, and inspire one another to thrive.
summary: Hello, we're EDAS! We provide a body-neutral space on campus and support for students struggling with food and body, as well as educational presentations. We provide preventative-based presentations, and peer-led support groups every Tuesday at 6:30!

name: Red Cross Club
description:
Red Cross at UO seeks to fulfill the mission of preventing and alleviating human suffering in the face of emergencies by mobilizing the power of volunteers and the generosity of donors. We do this through outreach, service projects, blood drives, and fundraising. We are affiliated with the Cascades Region of the American Red Cross. 

 

The Red Cross has multiple lines of service – disaster relief, blood, military & veteran communities, training, and international services. We aim to support all of these missions through the power of student engagement and community service.

 

Winter 2026 meetings on Mondays of even weeks in Esslinger 107 from 6-7 pm

 

Feel free to reach out to us with any questions on our instagram! @uo.red.cross
summary: Red Cross at UO seeks to fulfill the American Red Cross mission of preventing and alleviating human suffering in the face of emergencies. We do this through outreach, service projects, blood drives, and fundraising.

name: University of Oregon South Asian Law Student Association
summary: UOSALSA is an organization for South Asian students to get support to excel academically and professionally. This organization works closely with the South Asian Bar Association of Oregon, primarily to build a strong and well-connected community.

name: Pre-Pharmacy Society
description:
The Pre-Pharmacy Society is a student-led organization dedicated to supporting and guiding students interested in pursuing a career in pharmacy. Our mission is to create a welcoming and collaborative space where members can connect with like-minded peers, gain valuable insights into the pharmacy profession, and prepare for successful admission into pharmacy school.

We offer a variety of resources and events throughout the year, including guest speaker sessions with pharmacists and pharmacy school representatives, workshops on application strategies and interview preparation, and guidance on course selection and prerequisite planning. In addition to academic support, we strive to provide opportunities for hands-on experience and community involvement through volunteer projects, shadowing programs, and networking events.

Whether you’re just beginning to explore the field or are actively applying to pharmacy schools, the Pre-Pharmacy Society is here to help you build the knowledge, skills, and connections you need to thrive. Students of all majors and academic levels are encouraged to join. Whether you are exploring pharmacy as a potential career or preparing to apply to pharmacy school, the Pre-Pharmacy Society is here to support you every step of the way.
