Monday of Weeks 2, 4, 6, and 8 

4:00 pm 

Fenton 119 

(Dates: 10/6, 10/20, 11/3, 11/17) 

--

Contact Us! 

Instagram: uoprepharmacy 

Email: uoprepharmacy@gmail.com
summary: A space for students interested in pharmacy to connect, share resources, and support each other. Our goals include hosting guest speakers, offer application and course planning help, and create volunteering and networking opportunities.

name: Oregon Students for Law and Action
description: Oregon Students for Law and Action (OSLA) promotes student civic engagement through hands-on experience with law, policy, and government processes. Members will learn how to track and research bills aligned with their interests, learn to lobby and advocate for or against legislation and participate in trips to the Oregon Capitol to meet with lawmakers and representatives, sit in on live floor sessions and committee hearings. In addition to Capitol visits, members will engage in action-oriented events like policy-brief workshops, writing and creating op-eds and zines, etc. Students will gain skills in public speaking and testimony, legal research and legislative tracking, lobbying, advocacy, stakeholder analysis, writing, and confidence. Students from any and all majors are encouraged to join, especially those interested in law, politics, policymaking, government, public health, health equity, social justice, STEM, medicine, or environment. No prior experience required.
summary: Oregon Students for Law and Action (OSLA) is a student-led, non-partisan organization at the University of Oregon dedicated to taking student engagement beyond campus and into real-world policy-work through lobbying and advocacy

name: Wading Ducks
description:
Wading Ducks is a student-led fly fishing club built for those who love rivers, reels, and the quiet thrill of the cast. Whether you're a seasoned angler or just curious about the sport, we welcome everyone with an interest in fly fishing, conservation, and getting outside.

Our club hosts local outings to rivers and lakes around Oregon, offers fly-tying sessions, and meets regularly to talk gear, stories, and stream stewardship. We're also committed to protecting the waters we fish through clean-up events and conservation partnerships.
summary: Wading Ducks is a student fly fishing club that brings together anglers of all experience levels for fishing trips, fly-tying nights, and conservation activities around Oregon.

name: Lambda Sigma Gamma Sorority, Inc.
description: Lambda Sigma Gamma Sorority, Inc. is a multicultural sorority created to instill the desire for self-improvement, scholastic excellence, and the  cultivation for civic responsibility; also to promote unity and higher education amongst women.
summary: Lambda Sigma Gamma Sorority, Inc. is a multicultural sorority created to instill the desire for self-improvement, scholastic excellence, and the  cultivation for civic responsibility; also to promote unity and higher education amongst women.

name: Ki-Aikido Club at the University of Oregon
description: Ki-Aikido at University of Oregon is a co-ed sport that teaches and practices the traditional Japanese martial art of Shinshintoitsu Aikido. The club is directly affiliated with the Oregon Ki Society with the global headquarters located in Tokyo, Japan. The martial art of Aikido has a long history in Japan originating from Daito-ryu Aiki-jujutsu in the early 20th century, to now where the martial art is being taught to officers in Japan. Aikido is a sport that is open to anyone who wants to learn self-defense arts and learn the history and culture behind them.
summary: Aikido is a co-ed traditional martial arts/sport focusing on self-defense, softness and weapon arts as well as health exercises through practicing breathing/relaxation techniques. The club is noncompetitive and open to students of all levels!

name: Health Occupations Students of America - Future Health Professionals
description: HOSA aims to promote a diverse range of career opportunities in the healthcare industry, and advocates for the delivery of quality healthcare for all. Through leadership development, motivation, and recognition, it makes health science and biomedical science education accessible for students of all backgrounds. It also serves to address the acute shortage of qualified workers in the health care industry, and empowers members to become leaders in the global health community. HOSA offers 82 competitive events, ranging from skill-based to leadership and team-based. The event groups are as follows: Health science, health professions, leadership, and recognition events. Members compete at the regional, state, and international levels.
summary: HOSA promotes healthcare careers and quality care for all through biomedical science education for students of all backgrounds. It addresses the shortage of qualified medical workers and empowers members to become leaders in the global health community.

name: Simply Leadership
description:
The Simply Leadership club led by former Simply Youth Institute interns builds upon the foundation established by the nonprofit, Simply Youth Institute, mission to empower young adults through leadership development, responsibility, and inclusive collaboration.

The Simply Leadership club will encourage community collaboration, building leadership experience, and go beyond members’ creative limits through

  • Hands-on service projects 
  • Club mentorship 
  • Skill-building workshops

Core values of acceptance, creativity, and empowerment shapes the club’s culture, creating a welcoming space for students of all backgrounds. The chapter fosters interdisciplinary collaboration by inviting students from various majors and existing organizations on campus to contribute their unique talents toward shared goals. The Simply Leadership club will recruit members of University of Oregon organizations such as

  • Multicultural Center
  • Nontraditional Student Union
  • LGBTQA3

In alignment with Simply Youth Institute’s broader vision, this UO chapter will actively participate in local events, including Oregon fairs, existing and club-organized volunteer opportunities, and member-led community service projects. These efforts will not only strengthen community ties with Simply Youth Institute but also the University of Oregon. The Simply Leadership club also allow members to gain real-world experience in project organizing, networking, and public service.

With a focus on both personal and collective growth, the Simply Leadership club serves as a launchpad for students passionate about self improvement and uplifting community. By connecting driven young leaders to their communities, our club aims to leave a legacy of service, creativity, and inclusive leadership at the University of Oregon.
summary: The Simply Leadership club at the University of Oregon is a student-led initiative dedicated to cultivating the next generation of leaders through community engagement, mentorship, and creative expression.

name: Oregon Founders Club
description:
Oregon Founders Club is a student-led community dedicated to inspiring and supporting entrepreneurship at the University of Oregon. Our mission is to provide students with the resources, mentorship, and peer network needed to turn ideas into real ventures.

Through weekly meetings, workshops, and pitching support, members learn startup fundamentals, build teams, and receive feedback from experienced entrepreneurs. We connect students to alumni, local founders, and mentors while fostering a culture of collaboration and innovation on campus.

Whether members are exploring their first business idea, launching a startup, or simply curious about entrepreneurship, the Oregon Founders Club provides a supportive environment to learn, experiment, and grow.
summary: A student-led community empowering University of Oregon entrepreneurs through networking, mentorship, and hands-on support. We help founders launch ideas, learn startup skills, and connect with mentors and peers.

name: Connect @ University of Oregon
description: Connect@UO is the premier student-run networking organization at the University of Oregon, hosting twice per term networking mixers and various pop-up events for all majors. We strive to uplift the community, help students enhance their networking skills, and connect individuals with those they never thought they would meet.
summary: Connect@UO is the premier student-run networking organization at the University of Oregon, hosting twice per term networking mixers and various pop-up events for all majors. We strive to uplift the community, help students enhance their networking skills

name: Helping Hands: Student-led Support For The Unhoused Community
description:
Helping Hands is a student-led organization with an aim to support individuals experiencing homelessness in the Eugene Area, through creating care packages, outreach, and advocacy. Eugene has one of the highest per-capita rates of people experiencing homelessness in the United States. We feel passionate about responding to this with compassion, awareness, and supportive action.

 

Our primary goal is to meet immediate needs, through creating and distributing care packages full of daily necessities. We also recognize that homelessness is shaped by complex systems and networks. As a group, we aim not only to provide support directly, but also to engage in conversations and learning about the structural, political, and economic factors that contribute to housing insecurity. We hope to better understand these issues be part of creating meaningful, long-term solutions!
summary: Helping Hands is a student-led group at UO supporting people experiencing homelessness in Eugene through care packages, outreach, and advocacy, while raising awareness of the systems behind housing insecurity.

name: Books with Representation
description: In our club we want to read books that represent minority/the intersectionality of minority stories. Not only do we want to read these narratives, but discuss and create meaningful things based on them. From these readings there will be the ability to get to know each other more, spark creativity of creating our own art and stories, all while building an important community.
summary: A book club focusing on inclusive narratives. We are focusing on fiction and nonfiction that revolves around queer identities, BIPOC representation, mental health awareness, and disability representation. We want to read and make a community around it!

name: Legacy at UO
description: Legacy is a community for students in Sorority and Fraternity life to be bold about their faith. Legacy is a part of a parent organization called Cru, which has been active on college campuses for almost 70 years. We seek to provide a safe place for students from all backgrounds to learn about Jesus, grow in their faith, and love their fellow students and the University of Oregon. Through various activities we hold throughout the week, our goal is for students to better understand the Bible, engage in meaningful conversations about the intersection of life and faith, as well as have fun together in community.
summary: Legacy at UO is a student-led organization rooted in the FSL community that fosters non-denominational spirituality, connection and service.

name: OASIS
description:
OASIS: Oregon Alliance for Sustainable & Innovative Spaces

OASIS is dedicated to designing and implementing environmentally sustainable installations on campus and within the community. These projects consider the natural, social, historical, cultural, and physical aspects of each space.

Environmental design involves creating spaces that respect the ecosystem while promoting sustainability, resilience, and functionality across products, buildings, parks, and infrastructure.

Membership is open to all UO students, faculty, and local community volunteers, with special interest from disciplines such as ENVD, PPPM, ARCH, ART, IARC, ENVS, ENVL, GEOG, and more.
summary: Multi-disciplinary club that supports the new Environmental Design major by offering project work, community partnership and networking opportunities.

name: Occupy Medical
description:
About Occupy Medical 

Occupy Medical is a 100% volunteer-run, 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization composed of healthcare professionals, support personnel, and community members, who believe that healthcare is a basic human right. Our mission is to make healthcare accessible to all—regardless of housing status, income, or insurance—by removing barriers to care and meeting people where they are.

At our weekly free clinics in Eugene, we provide a variety of services, including:

  • Basic medical care from licensed healthcare professionals.
  • Supplies handouts, including wound care kits, hygiene products, and clothing.
  • Haircut services

Our Mission at the University of Oregon

The UO student chapter of Occupy Medical serves as a bridge between the University of Oregon community and the greater Eugene/Springfield population. Our goals include:

  • Educating students about the realities and challenges faced by the local unhoused community.
  • Promoting awareness of the social determinants of health, including housing, education, income, and systemic inequity.
  • Encouraging interdisciplinary dialogue around public health, medicine, social work, and policy.
  • Fostering a culture of compassionate service and community-based action.

Through our club, UO students from all majors and backgrounds are invited to engage with meaningful, hands-on experiences that explore the intersection of healthcare, justice, and community wellness.

 

MEETING INFO:

Board Meetings: Every 1st Thursday of the month @ 5:30 PM

Club Meetings: Every 3rd Thursday of the month @ 5:30 PM

> Meeting locations vary: will be listed on emailed reminders and Engage posts.

> Email occmednow@gmail.com to receive monthly newsletters & meeting reminders!

> Everyone is welcome to attend!
summary: Occupy Medical is a 100% volunteer-run, 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization striving to improve access to health care for the underserved Eugene/Springfield community.

name: Oregon Business Law Association
description: Our club explores the intersection of business and law, helping students understand how legal principles shape real-world business decisions. We host weekly meetings featuring case studies, discussions on current legal issues, and collaborative projects that prepare members for careers in business, law, or compliance. Members participate in guest speaker events, networking opportunities, and workshops on topics like contracts, ethics, and entrepreneurship. Our goal is to create an inclusive space where students can develop their knowledge of business law, build professional skills, and engage with the UO community.
summary: Bridges the gap between business and legal education, offering workshops, case studies and guest speakers to prepare students for careers in law, consulting and finance.

name: Pre-Eye Health Club
summary: This club connects students pursuing careers in Optometry, Ophthalmology, PA, and any other eye-health-related fields. We are both an educational and community-building resource that is open to anyone interested.

name: Shakesqueer
summary: Explores queer presence in theatre, particularly through Shakespeare, creating space for discussion, performance and community.

name: Forensics at the University of Oregon
description:
The goal of Forensics at UO is to uphold the values and practices of the historical iterations of forensics programs on the University of Oregon campus. The program consists of Debate and Mock Trial. 

 

Forensics at UO traces its history from a debating society formed in 1876 within weeks of the University of Oregon’s founding. Since then, various iterations of forensics activities on campus have become emblematic of the university’s broader effort to produce well-educated graduates, ready to become active and involved members of society. 

 

Forensics at UO is a space for students to practice their argumentation, research, and public speaking skills, while educating them on important social, political, and economic affairs, and matters of the law.

 

Follow us on Instagram: @oregondebate @uomocktrial
summary: Forensics at the University of Oregon consists of two branches: Debate and Mock Trial, promoting public speaking, argumentation, and research skills, alongside a better understanding of the law. Reach out for practice time and places!

name: Kappa Alpha Pi
description:
On October 8, 2007, a group of determined pre-law students at the University of Michigan established Kappa Alpha Pi (KAPi), a fraternity dedicated to fostering academic excellence and professional development. Since its inception, the organization has expanded to include chapters nationwide, providing a supportive network for aspiring legal professionals to thrive and succeed in their chosen field.

Kappa Alpha Pi Pre-Law and Government Gender Inclusive Professional Fraternity exists nationally to foster a gender-inclusive brotherhood for undergraduate students interested in law and government. Our brotherhood is open to all undergraduate focuses of study. Chapters are devoted to developing students professionally and academically to be leaders in the world that follows graduation.
summary: Kappa Alpha Pi Pre-Law and Government Gender Inclusive Professional Fraternity exists nationally to foster an inclusive brotherhood for undergraduate students interested in law and government.

name: Women's Accounting and Finance Club
description: A community for students interested in Accounting and Finance, with a special focus on supporting women and marginalized groups. The Women’s Accounting and Finance Club (WAFC) strives to create a welcoming and inclusive space where members can connect, collaborate, and grow. We offer a relaxed environment that encourages creativity, confidence, and meaningful connections within and beyond the field.
summary: Please update the description of your organization

name: Duck's Art and Technology Club
description: Duck's Art and Technology Club provides a space for learning, first and foremost. With meetings focused on sharing the cool art club members create, and events focused on learning skills outside of your comfort zone, we aim to help every DATTIE make! No experience is too limited, no medium is too niche. Come by and show your work!
summary: Duck's Art and Technology Club provides a space for artists of all experience levels to meet the people and learn the skills necessary to bring their ideas to life!

name: Turning Point USA at UO
description:
Turning Point USA is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization founded in 2012 by Charlie Kirk. The organization’s mission is to identify, educate, train, and organize students to promote the principles of fiscal responsibility, free markets, and limited government. 

Turning Point USA, Inc., is organized and operated exclusively for educational and charitable purposes in accordance with Section 501(c)(3), to empower informed civic and cultural engagement grounded in American exceptionalism and a positive spirit of action.

Turning Point USA guides citizens through development of knowledge, skills, values, and motivation, so they can meaningfully engage in their communities to restore traditional American values like patriotism, respect for life, liberty, family, and fiscal responsibility.
summary: TPUSA’s student program exists to educate young people about the importance of limited government, free markets, and freedom. Students are encouraged to be leaders in their communities. TPUSA is a social club and not affiliated with a political party.

name: EnVi Crochet 501(c)(3)
description:
Looking to add a creative twist to community service? ��

EnVi Crochet is a student-led 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization dedicated to promoting environmental sustainability and public health through crochet. Founded in Portland, Oregon in 2022, EnVi has already planted over 4000 trees worldwide through funds raised by selling crochet bouquets. 

As we launch a new chapter at the University of Oregon, we're excited to welcome passionate students ready to make a difference through creativity. No crochet experience? No problem—we value a wide range of skill sets, including marketing, sales, and social media.

EnVi Crochet is organized into three teams:

  • Production Team: Meets weekly in person to crochet and assemble flowers
  • Sales Team: Vends at markets and community events
  • Publicity Team: Promotes EnVi through outreach, social media, and photoshoots

Our impact has been recognized through four news features and the Beaverton MYAB Rising Entrepreneurs Award. Join us in our mission to create a greener world, one stitch at a time. 🧶💚
summary: Looking to add a creative twist to community service? 🌱 EnVi is a 501c3 nonprofit that sells crochet bouquets to fund tree planting. No crochet experience needed—join our Production, Sales, or Publicity Team and make a difference, one stitch at a time!

name: Litter Walk Club
description: Pods of club members will be given a trash bag and a litter picker and sent on planned routes around Eug to take scenic walks and leave the world better than we found it. Routes will be planned and divided into teams depending on who signs up for the day.
summary: Club members are split into small groups and walk or roll a planned route around eugene, picking up any trash they find along the way. Exercise and good deeds in one!

name: Fishing Club
description:
Our goal is to get as many UO students out fishing and enjoying nature as possible through building a strong community of anglers and hosting educational meetings about where and how to catch fish in Oregon. Fishing club is open to all experience levels(beginners encouraged) and is the best place to meet new fishing buddies and get tips on gear and techniques

We meet once every term to discuss all the fishing opportunity's for the season and the best techniques to use. We also have a range of other meetings/classes on specific topics or skills within fishing  

For more info about club events and activities check out the club instagram @UOfishingclub and join the discord to stay connected. If you have any questions please hit up the club email or dm the instagram.

Hope to see you on the river! 

discord:https://discord.gg/WHcrGXscyC

Instagram:https://www.instagram.com/ducks_fishing_club?igsh=MW9vYnZkZGxpZjBqbg%3D%3D&utm_source=qr
summary: Our goal is to get as many UO students out fishing and enjoying nature as possible. Fishing club is open to all experience levels(beginners encouraged) and is the best place to meet new fishing buddies and get tips on gear and techniques

name: Accessible Education Center
description:
The Accessible Education Center (AEC) is part of the Undergraduate Education and Student Success Division of Academic Affairs. Our name reflects our broad scope of attention and an emphasis on systemic change to increase accessibility and inclusivity across campus.  

The University of Oregon is dedicated to diversity, equity, inclusion, and equal opportunity in education. Disability is recognized as an aspect of diversity integral to the university and to society. We know that our university flourishes when the contributions of students with disabilities are valued. Students with disabilities can often ask questions that nondisabled people can’t articulate, offer perspectives that deepen understanding of complex human experiences and problems, and challenge fundamental assumptions of power and belonging in every academic discipline and field. Yet disability has historically been excluded, ignored, and/or stigmatized in the traditional university environments.  

The AEC is dedicated to facilitating and supporting accessible education through access and full inclusion of students with disabilities into the university environment. This is accomplished through effective collaborations/consultations with faculty/staff, proactive advising, and the fostering of systemic campus change. The AEC works to create and sustain physical, curricular, and informational environments that are informed by and responsive to the diverse characteristics and experiences of students with disabilities.
summary: The AEC is dedicated to facilitating and supporting accessible education through access and full inclusion of students with disabilities into the university environment.

name: Planned Parenthood Generation Action at University of Oregon
description:
Planned Parenthood Generation Action is a network of young activists across the country who organize events on their campuses and in their communities to mobilize advocates for reproductive freedom and raise public awareness about reproductive health.

 

Meeting Location: McKenzie 101 @ 5:00 PM. Every other Thursday: 1/15, 1/29, 2/12, 2/26, 3/12
summary: Planned Parenthood Generation Action is a network of young activists across the country who organize events on their campuses and in their communities to mobilize advocates for reproductive freedom and raise public awareness about reproductive health.

name: Plant Futures at UO
description: University of Oregon Plant Futures Chapter is open to students of all levels and academic programs. The University of Oregon Chapter focuses on education and multi-disciplinary learning around building sustainable, just, and plant-centric food systems. Beyond education and community building, another core focus of the chapter is to serve as a space for members to secure meaningful professional development opportunities, mentorship, and career opportunities within plant-centric food systems. Students will be able to connect with nonprofit and for-profit organizations addressing exciting opportunities within the sector.
summary: Plant Futures at University of Oregon focuses on education and multi-disciplinary learning around building sustainable, just, and plant-centric food systems while serving as a space and catalyst for professional development within this field.

name: Hearts for the Homeless Eugene
description:
Hearts for the Homeless (H4H) Eugene is an organization that serves to provide one of the most vulnerable populations with free blood pressure screenings, education on the causes of high blood pressure, and clinical outreach programs. Through partnering with local food drives and community health clinics, H4H strives to reduce the prevalence of cardiovascular disease in the homeless community of Eugene/Springfield.

Furthermore, Hearts for the Homeless seeks to educate its members about cardiovascular disease, patient interaction, and hidden difficulties in the homeless community through volunteer experience. This organization assists with shaping the minds of our members into compassionate leaders along with spreading awareness of cardiovascular disease to underprivileged communities in Eugene. Our mission is to promote dignity and well being through community based medical service and outreach.
summary: Hearts for the Homeless (H4H) Eugene is an organization that provides the homeless population with free blood pressure screenings, education on the causes of high blood pressure, and clinical outreach programs.

name: Teddy Bear Hospital
description: Teddy Bear Hospital USA (TBH) is a non profit organization founded in 2022 that's main goal is to reduce clinical anxiety and promote medical literacy in young children by creating fun, positive experiences with healthcare. Led by undergraduates, teddy bear hospital bridges the gap between university and public community, through interactive, hands on "mock hospital sessions" at local schools, teaching kids to treat their own teddy bears, learning about patient care and healthcare concepts in a relaxed atmosphere, reducing medical stigma and anxiety, a common struggle for children. Teddy Bear Hospital uses three main pillars to describe their mission: Medical Literacy, by introducing concepts through light hearted descriptions using teddy bears as examples, Community, by connecting children with medicine and, Well Being, by easing fears associated with doctors and hospitals. Teddy Bear Hospital also provides valuable training for pre med or elementary education focused students, allowing them to practice pediatric/elementary communication skills and build rapport with children.
summary: Teddy Bear Hospital USA is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit that aims to increase medical literacy and health well-being with younger children to reduce their fears of medical environments.

name: Plant Club
description: Plant Club is social and educational experience to engage students, centered around a love and fascination of plants. An opportunity for promoting mental health, the plant club provides a safe, relaxing, and restorative space for students to connect over their shared interest. The plant club will curate and maintain a small exotic plant collection at the UO Research Greenhouse Facility and learn/teach plant care skills. Members will learn plant care skills they will carry with them through life beyond school. Plant Club looks forward to collaborating with other environmentally minded clubs.
summary: On-campus club for UO students to connect over their shared interest of plants. This engaging, social, and educational club will provide members the opportunity to learn more about plants, get hands-on experience, and promote better mental health.

name: Eugene Goth Club
description: The goth club will take weekly,4x a month and we will discuss gothic literature as well as art, music, icons and more. We plan presentations as well as group activities and get to know you games. There will also be opportunities to connect to the further Eugene and Oregon goth communities, going as far as coordinating trips to Portland for their many goth communities. We meet in emu rooms that are posted in the discord and on the instagram.
summary: We will host meetings twice a month to discuss gothic literature, music, art and icons as well as plan outside events to connect with the local goth community.

name: Pacific Island Student Alliance
description: PISA seeks to create and steward a space where Indigenous Pacific Islander students and allies at the University of Oregon can learn, celebrate their cultures, and build solidarity across all Pasifika identities. In 2025, we honor two decades of history by reigniting the spirit of past Pacific Islander student organizations and building a stronger, more unified Pasifika community for the future.
summary: PISA is dedicated to serving, educating, and uplifting Melanesian, Micronesian, and Polynesian students at the University of Oregon by fostering a dedicated, enduring, and loving community that celebrates identity, culture, and belonging.

name: Collegiate Challenge
description: This organizations objective is to recruit members and raise funds for a trip to Medford over the 2026 Spring Break. This trip will be for 10 architecture students, and they will spend five days volunteering on an affordable housing project under the guidance of Habitat for Humanity. The organization will also share resources and set a path forward for underclassmen to continue the tradition annually.
summary: This is a student organization that plans and fundraises for Collegiate Challenges with Habitat for Humanity.

name: Country Western Dance Club at UO
description: The Country Western Dance Club at UO is a student-led organization dedicated to celebrating and sharing the traditions of dance and country western culture. We welcome students from all backgrounds who are interested in learning partner and line dances to country music, in addition to other genres. Whether you're a seasoned two-stepper or an absolute beginner with a curiosity about line dancing, our club offers an open, friendly space to connect with others, build skill, and have fun.
summary: The Country Western Dance Club at UO is a student-led group celebrating country western culture through partner and line dancing, socials, and lessons. All are welcome—no experience needed. Join us to dance, connect, and have fun!

name: Women in Politics at UO
description: The Women in Politics club is an academic club that provides a safe and open space for students to be educated on, openly research, and have conversations about current political events. We provide a nonpartisan approach to every topic and focus on educating our members solely based on evidence. Members do not have to be political science majors or have any previous experience or knowledge of politics, this club is structured around learning the basics for those who want to begin their political education and literacy. By providing reliable sources and data, we are able to educate both ourselves and our members. On a larger scale, our mission is to get more people knowledgeable and involved with their local governments, specifically Eugene and Lane County.
summary: The Women in Politics club is an academic club that provides a safe and open space for students to be educated on, openly research, and have conversations about current political events in a non partisan environment.

name: Women In Media
description: WIM is a space for those who want to be seen, heard, and impossible to ignore. This is designed for women entering the media, journalism, and communication industries to build visibility, confidence, and professional readiness through community, workshops, mentorship, and speaker events.
summary: WIM is a space for those who want to be seen, heard, and impossible to ignore, achieved through community, workshops, mentorship, and speaker events that build visibility, confidence, and professional readiness

name: TALK@UO
description: The average day of a college student consists of going to classes, working if they have a job, some physical activity, seeing the same friend group, and consuming social media or a TV show. While there's nothing wrong with that, it seems like we've lost something along the way. There are too few meaningful conversations between friends. It seems like the only way to meet people is by partying. "TALK@UO" looks to bring an open and informal community to UO and Eugene, with the sole purpose of talking to others. The club offers public speaking and networking opportunities, along with genuine discourse and meaningful connections. At the end of the day, we all just want to experience a fun environment and community where we belong—that's what TALK@UO brings.
summary: Bringing back the lost "3rd place", TALK@UO provides a space to have both casual and serious conversations with new people. Meetings and events enhance interpersonal skills while fostering a sense of community that many young people yearn for.

name: Software in Business Club
summary: Our mission is to provide a community for students interested in software development, programming, and technology, while connecting members with networking, internships, and career opportunities in the technology field.

name: Omega Delta Phi
summary: The purpose of this Brotherhood, a Service/Social fraternity dedicated to the needs and concerns of the community, is and shall be to promote and maintain the traditional values of Unity, Honesty, Integrity, and Leadership.

name: The Whimsy Club
description: The Whimsy Club is a social club that meets at Unthank 264 From 6-7 each Thursday. Our purpose is to create a whimsical and stress-free environment where anyone can make new friends and have new, fun experiences around campus. Every week there will be a new activity that we do that is suggested and voted on by the members. Some examples include an arts and crafts night, nature walk, a giant game of uno, Mario Kart Night, and much more!
summary: The Whimsy Club is a social club centered around bringing whimsy and childlike wonder back onto campus. The club is open to everyone with an emphasis on making new friends and creating new, fun experiences around campus. We meet Thursdays 6-7 @Unthank264

name: Swing Club at UO
description:
We meet every other Thursday night for lindy hop dancing! This club serves as a bridge between dancers at UO and track town swing. At every meeting we will have a one hour lesson with a social dance following.

Beginners and experienced dancers are welcome and no partner is needed. This is a great way to learn new moves and meet other students!

Check out our instagram for more info! @swingclubatuo
summary: In this club, dances will be held on campus every other week to all students free of charge in the lindy hop swing style. The dance will include a lesson and a social dance.

name: Citizens' Climate Lobby
description:
Citizens’ Climate Lobby is a climate change organization that exists to create the political will for a livable world by enabling individual breakthroughs in the exercise of personal and political power.

Our consistently respectful, nonpartisan approach to climate education is designed to create a broad, sustainable foundation to drive climate action across all geographic regions and political inclinations. By building upon shared values rather than partisan divides, and empowering our supporters to work in keeping with the concerns of their local communities, we work towards the adoption of fair, effective, and sustainable climate change solutions.

We work to support volunteers and build relationships with elected officials, the media and their local community.

CCL volunteers are organized into hundreds of local chapters across the US and internationally. These chapters build political support for climate action with a variety of tools, which they use in keeping with their local culture and politics. By focusing on shared values rather than partisan divides, we build relationships with community leaders and with federal elected officials and with Congress, always starting from a place of respect, gratitude, and appreciation.
summary: We are a University chapter of the larger nonprofit, nonpartisan, grassroots advocacy climate change organization focused on national policies to address the national and global climate crisis.

name: Immigration Law Student Association
description: ILSA strives to educate students and community members about the complex struggles and nature of immigration law, while creating a safe environment for all involved.
summary: Immigration Law Student Association

name: English Graduate Organization
summary: The English Graduate Organization (EGO) is a student-led body that serves to foster community, advocacy, and professional development among graduate students in the Department of English.

name: Society of Physics Students at UO
summary: Society of Physics Students at UO

name: Screen Consciousness Club Oregon
description:
Our organization aims to reduce the amount of unconscious screentime through providing both tools to help people manage their usage. The club also aims to provide activities in an off-screen environment, and will, on top of presenting research and methods to reduce usage, spend most of the club time playing games and other fun social activities to come and engage in off of their phones.
You are welcome to show up for either part of the club!

We meet every other Monday (Odd weeks) at 5:30pm in Anstett 193!
summary: Helping people reduce and realize unconscious screen usage within daily life.

name: First Generation College Grads
summary: First Gen College Grads

name: Brazilian Student Association at the University of Oregon
description: The Brazilian Student Association at the University of Oregon (BRASA UO) is a student-led cultural organization dedicated to fostering community, cultural exchange, and professional development among Brazilian students, Brazilian-American students, and those interested in Brazilian culture. BRASA UO provides a welcoming space for connection through cultural events, language practice, social gatherings, and educational programming. The organization aims to strengthen Brazilian representation on campus, promote inclusivity, and support students’ personal, academic, and professional growth.
summary: BRASA UO is a student-led cultural organization that connects Brazilian and Brazilian-American students and promotes Brazilian culture through community events, language exchange, and educational programming.

name: Oregon Lens
description:
Oregon Lens at the University of Oregon is to create a welcoming space where students can connect, share their work, and grow together as photographers. Whether you’re shooting on a digital, film, or your phone, our club is open to anyone interested in exploring photography, no experience required. Because photography classes can be hard to access or fill up quickly, this club is designed to give students an alternative way to learn, practice, and build creative community outside the classroom. We’ll host weekly meetings focused on different styles and techniques of photography, along with:

  • Photo walks

  • workshops

  • Guest speakers

  • Creative challenges

  • Portfolio sharing and feedback

  • Showcases and field trips as the club grows

Our goal is to make photography more accessible to students and allow them to express themselves artistically.
summary: Oregon Lens is to create a welcoming space where students can connect, share their work, and grow together as photographers. Whether you’re shooting on a digital, film, or your smartphone. Open to all skill levels to create creativity.

name: Sewing Club at the University of Oregon
description: Sew@UO is a student-led sewing club that promotes sewing as a great hobby to pick up. The goal for Sew@UO is get more people sewing and share knowledge about sewing skills others may not know. This could be teaching our club members about garment construction, pattern making, and hand stitching techniques.
summary: Sew@UO is a club that promotes education and creative production of clothes through sewing and hand stitching. The club aims to get more people to sew and expand their existing sewing knowledge about sewing and garment construction.

name: VAW Global Health Alliances
description: VAW develops community health systems while providing interns the opportunity to work with healthcare professionals in low to middle-income countries. Interns will learn about healthcare in a global context through shadowing local physicians and learn about disease diagnosis, treatment courses, clinic operations, and basic pharmacology.
summary: VAW develops community health systems while providing interns the opportunity to work alongside healthcare professionals. Interns will work closely with community partners to implement primary medical clinics across low and middle-income countries.

name: Sports Medicine Club at University of Oregon
shortName: Sports Med Club at UO, SPM at UO
description: Our organization aims to educate and connect students with different career paths in sports medicine. Students interested in medicine with a focus on sports and athletics.
summary: Our organization aims to educate and connect students with different career paths in sports medicine. Students interested in medicine with a focus on sports and athletics.

name: Latinos in Business
description: A business organization that empowers and inspires the Latinx community through professional development, leadership opportunities, and weekly workshops featuring Latinx speakers and panels, all grounded in cultural connection and community building.
summary: A business organization that empowers and inspires the Latinx community through professional development, leadership opportunities, and weekly workshops featuring Latinx speakers and panels, all grounded in cultural connection and community building.
